  • Company/contact signal is present, so this can go into outreach review.
Full job description

We are a Danish marketing agency specializing in Google Ads for Danish and international clients. Our portfolio is roughly split between lead-generation and ecommerce, and we are building toward becoming the best lead-gen Google Ads agency in Denmark with plans to scale internationally. We work with accounts spending between $600/month and $20,000/month today, and we expect to onboard accounts spending $50,000 to $100,000/month in the near future. You shall not be afraid of accounts at that scale. We already have 2 Filipino team members, so you would not be the first. We are looking at the BEST Google Ads specialists OnlineJobs.ph has to offer. If you are top 1-5% of your field, please read on. If you are a generalist who also does Google Ads on the side, please skip this post, lets save each others time. This is a senior-level, narrow-specialist role. We work in both Danish and English. You do NOT need to speak Danish, but you must be sharp enough to BUILD SYSTEMS using AI tools that let you do keyword research, search term analysis, and ad copy review across languages you do not personally speak. THE NARROW FOCUS: We do NOT need Meta Ads, email marketing, SEO, social media, web design, content writing, or full-stack digital marketing. The less you do besides Google Ads, the better fit you are. We already have a web designer, I have a VA, and I have a general marketing guy for the rest. We need someone uncompromisingly sharp at Google Ads and only Google Ads. We do not train you in Google Ads. We expect you to know it 100% from day one and to challenge me (Rasmus) when you think I am wrong. If you can only answer Google Ads questions by asking an AI, you are not the right fit. AI is your augmentation, not your fundament. THE VISION FOR THIS ROLE: In the beginning, I (Rasmus) will build account strategy together with you. Over time you take on more accounts independently and eventually own direct client contact. I will handle less and less of the day-to-day so I can focus on scaling the company, optimizing our Google Ads workflows, and expanding our automation systems. The goal is for you to grow into a role where you: - Talk directly with German (our web developer) to brief and build relevant landing pages for your accounts - Set up offline conversion tracking (WhatConverts integrated with each client's CRM) - Manage Google Ads 100% on your own, making the right decisions and able to articulate WHY for each one - Log every account action as a hypothesis in our internal AI cockpit (we track what worked, what did not, and why, so that we later have a backlog of we did this, it worked, why/why not) - Help me develop more internal tools that speed up our processes - Spot bottlenecks across our workflow and propose fixes - Keep a finger on the pulse of the industry (LinkedIn, podcasts, whatever your channel is) and bring new ideas in, especially around AI, the Google Ads API, and the way Google Ads is evolving - Tell the team, when we're wasting our time, and could do something better with our time - Feel uncomfortable whenever you're wasting time, or could optimize a proces further I need someone who is not falling behind. AI, the Google Ads API, and the Google Ads platform itself are all moving extremely fast in 2026. Standing still equals falling backward. The right person grows into a true partner on the Google Ads side of the business. HOW WE WORK (philosophy): We automate audits, account management, monitoring alerts, and reporting. Not primarily to make things faster, but to INCREASE the quality every client receives. Quality first, speed second. We work extensively through the Google Ads API and expect you to grow into that work too. We track profit (not just ROAS) for every client we can. Profit is the north star. ABOUT YOUR ACCOUNTS: You will manage between 10 and 30 client accounts depending on size. Smaller accounts may only need a few hours per month. Larger accounts will need 10+ hours per month. You schedule your own time, manage your own deadlines, and proactively tell us when you need more hours allocated to deliver quality work. We do NOT chase you. You own your portfolio, and I need someone I can trust, so I never have to checkup. If I need to check up, then you're no the right person. You'll get a few accounts to test on in the beginning as we onboard, then slowly a bigger and bigger part of the portfolio. Tasks and responsibilities: - Own 10 to 30 client accounts across lead-gen and ecommerce (execution, optimization, reporting) - Build and refine campaigns (Search, Performance Max, Shopping) - Manage Google Merchant Center feeds and Shopping for e-com clients - Light conversion tracking work (we use ProfitMetrics, Reaktion, WhatConverts which are plug-and-play) - Search terms mining at scale across languages using AI-driven systems you build - Optimize Smart Bidding based on signal quality, diagnose underperformers holistically - Help us improve our automation systems and internal tooling - Log decisions as hypotheses in our AI cockpit so we can track what works - Collaborate via Loom, Slack, and Productive.io with time-tracked reporting - Schedule your own work and proactively flag when you need more hours allocated Minimum requirements: - Fluent in written and spoken English. We cannot have language barriers at this level - 5+ years hands-on Google Ads, agency-side - Has managed 3+ accounts for 12+ months with concrete before/after numbers in your portfolio. I expect you to have enough experience to be able to draw parallels to previous accounts, niches, setups etc. if you can't, you don't have enough experience - Comfortable operating at $50,000+ monthly ad spend levels - Can walk us through specific past accounts in depth: what was the strategy, who made it, why that decision, what was the result, and why did it happen. We want to see your thinking, not just your wins. - Deep knowledge of Smart Bidding, Performance Max (including native brand exclusion), Google Shopping, Merchant Center feed optimization, Google Ads Scripts, GAQL - Talks in POAS (profit on ad spend) and LTGP (lifetime gross profit), not only ROAS - AI-augmented, not AI-dependent. Daily Claude/ChatGPT use, but answers Google Ads questions from your own knowledge - Has personally built at least one workflow automation (Google Ads Script, Python, Sheets, Make/Zapier) - Comfortable with the Google Ads API or willing to grow into it fast - Stays current with industry developments (AI, the API, Google Ads platform changes) - Self-scheduler. Owns deadlines. Proactively communicates when more time is needed. - Long-term mindset, not stepping-stone seeker Our main tools (all provided by us): - Ads stack: Google Ads, Google Ads API, GA4, Google Tag Manager, Google Merchant Center, Channable - Conversion tracking: ProfitMetrics, Reaktion, WhatConverts - Project management: Productive.io - Communication: Google Workspace (your own account), Slack, Loom - AI: Claude Pro Max (multiple licenses), Gemini, ChatGPT - Any other software you need to do the job well, within reason Compensation: - Base starts at $20/hour. Final rate depends on what you bring (agreed after we have seen your work). - Revenue share: percentage of monthly revenue from each client account you own. When your client grows, your income grows. - Retainer bonus: percentage of retainer fee for each client you successfully retain (structured with the right person) - Performance bonuses paid quarterly for retention and growth wins - Paid in advance monthly (you NEVER chase invoices). Later I can also pay 3 months in advance if the employee wants that. - 2 weeks paid vacation per year + paid sick days, after 90-day trial passes. - 13th month consideration built into total comp - Paid learning time included We track real, focused hours only (not screen-on time). 25-30 tracked hours per week equates to a 35-40 hour work week with breaks included. Hiring process: Step 1: Apply via this post with the items listed at the bottom. Step 2: If shortlisted, record a short Loom video introducing yourself, your portfolio, and your perspective on Google Ads work. Step 3: We exchange a few messages to dig deeper. Step 4: You fill out a Google Form with specific case study questions. Step 5: 30-45 minute video call with Kevin (our hiring manager). Step 6: 30-45 minute video call with Rasmus (CEO). Goal: right person onboarded within 1-2 months. How We Work: Weekly Meetings (later biweekly and eventually monthly) - One 30-minute meeting per week to start. Once you are settled in, we move to biweekly and eventually monthly. Beyond that, most work is async via Loom and Slack. Easy Communication - Daily back-and-forth via Loom videos and screen recordings to save time and ensure clarity. We expect you can take a client call if needed, but we do very little live calling. Detailed Reporting - After each task, provide reporting/screenshots/videos along with exact time-tracking timestamps. All reporting is billable. Cultural & Work-Mentality Expectations: Open Communication & Solution Focus - In Denmark, admitting when you are unsure or need help lets us troubleshoot together quickly. We need you to disagree with us when you have data. Flat Hierarchy & Direct Feedback - Low power distance: feel free to push back, give and receive feedback directly and constructively. Drop "Sir" and "Ma'am" early. Supportive & Honest Environment - We never shout, yell, or curse. Growth happens fastest when every opinion is heard and all feedback is delivered respectfully. Quality Over Quantity & 80/20 Principle - We prioritize doing it right the first time to minimize revisions. Focus on the 20% of effort that delivers 80% of the impact. Initiative & Independence - While support is always available, we value specialists who proactively identify opportunities, ask smart questions, suggest improvements, and tell us early when they need more time to deliver quality. Time-Zone Flexibility - Collaboration is primarily in Central European Time (UTC+1/UTC+2). You do not need to cover any specific hours. Rasmus, our CEO, is in Asia 9/10 months per year as well. Only 1 meeting per week is fixed (later biweekly/monthly). Respectful Tone - Directness coupled with respect: clear language, polite interactions, friendly meeting culture. Future Growth Opportunities: - Path to full-time within within 3-6 months months based on performance and mutual fit. Right now we can't get more clients because of capacity, when that has been freed up, we will onboard more, and I need someone that can allocate more time till we hit full-time. - Revenue share grows as you take on more accounts - Retainer bonus grows with client retention - Direct client contact and account ownership grows over time - Potential to lead a small team as we scale toward becoming the best lead-gen Google Ads agency in Denmark and beyond - We train you in our task management system, How We Work, AI/Claude workflows, and automation systems. We do NOT train you in Google Ads. How to apply: Reply with the following in this order: 1. Start your reply with the word "POAS" as the first word (shows you read carefully) 2. One paragraph about the most recent Google Ads account you moved meaningfully. Include before/after numbers and what specifically YOU changed, the more nerdy and specific the better, but don't write a bible to me please. 3. Link to your portfolio or case studies 4. Link to your LinkedIn profile if you have one 5. One sentence on which AI tool you use daily and for what 6. One link to a workflow, script, or automation you have built 7. Your current weekly availability starting July or August 2026 Applications missing any of the above will not be reviewed. Thank you for your interest.

Full job description

We're hiring autonomous React Native / Expo developers to ship mobile apps for an established mobile product agency (Keenov). This role is different from most. We don't need you to architect from a blank page - we hand you a clear, detailed roadmap and a ready-made AI development setup, configured and documented. Your job is to execute with judgment: drive the AI well, move fast, and feel when something is built right versus when it just looks done. We care far less about years of experience than about your eye for quality. You're probably a fit if you: - Have shipped at least one app to the App Store and/or Google Play - Know React Native + Expo and can take an app to a TestFlight build yourself (EAS Build, signing) - Use AI coding tools daily (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot), with opinions on where they help and where they lie - Work best with a clear target and full autonomy What you get: - A real pipeline, not a one-off gig - recurring projects - A system that makes you fast: complete apps in weeks, not months - Full autonomy, fully async, work from anywhere - Pay: a fixed 750-1,000 EUR per complete app (spec to TestFlight), plus a 1,000 EUR bonus when you run several in parallel How it works: if there's a fit, we send you a small paid test task - one screen, shipped to TestFlight - so we can see how you actually work. To apply, send your application through OnlineJobs.ph with a link to one app you've shipped and the AI tools you use daily.

Senior IT Engineer Prevent problems before they happen. Automate what shouldn’t be done twice. Do great work with great clients — and earn the autonomy to do it your way. To apply please fill out the application form here: u247.us/bepartofourteam. About the...

Full job description

Senior IT Engineer Prevent problems before they happen. Automate what shouldn’t be done twice. Do great work with great clients — and earn the autonomy to do it your way. To apply please fill out the application form here: u247.us/bepartofourteam. About the Company We’re a family-owned business with all-local Philippines leadership and team. We’ve been 100% work-from-home since day one — not a pandemic pivot. We’ve grown by valuing our people, building long-term relationships, and doing great work for great clients. The Role You’re not here to close tickets faster. You’re here to make sure the same problem doesn’t come back — and ideally, to catch it before it ever reaches a ticket queue. We’re looking for a Senior IT Engineer who thinks like an automator and operator first, a firefighter second. You’ll handle complex Tier 2/3 escalations when they arise, but your real work is building the systems, automations, and processes that prevent issues from happening in the first place. You’ll own client environments, lead from the front technically, and raise the standard of the team around you. The Mindset We’re Looking For The best engineers we’ve worked with share a few traits: They hate doing the same thing twice. If they fix something manually, their next thought is: “How do I make sure no one — including me — has to do this again?” They take ownership. From the moment a problem is theirs, it’s theirs until it’s fully resolved, documented, and won’t come back They think upstream. Instead of asking “how do I fix this faster?” they ask “why is this happening, and how do I prevent it?” They communicate clearly. Status, blockers, and resolutions are shared openly — before being asked for They keep learning. Twelve months from now, they know more than they do today — without anyone telling them to Who Thrives Here People who want to do genuinely good work, be trusted by their team, and build something that lasts. We move fast and hold a high bar — but we’ve also built an environment where people are respected, given real ownership, and invested in over time. Those things aren’t in conflict here. We’re a growing MSP, which means not every process is fully documented and not every system is perfect yet. For the right engineer, that’s not a problem — it’s the opportunity to shape how we operate, not just execute within it. What You’ll Do You’ll walk into a fast-moving MSP environment where clients depend on you and junior engineers look to you. Your job isn’t just to solve the problem in front of you — it’s to make sure the team and the environment are better because you were there. That looks like: Serving as the Tier 3 escalation point for complex issues across servers, networks, cloud platforms, and endpoint security — diagnosing root causes, not patching symptoms Proactively auditing client environments to catch risks, vulnerabilities, and inefficiencies before they become incidents Building monitoring, alerting, and automation that catches problems early — or eliminates them entirely Leading infrastructure projects end-to-end — cloud migrations, M365 deployments, network overhauls, security implementations — from scoping through delivery Writing and maintaining PowerShell scripts that turn repetitive manual tasks into automated, reliable processes Mentoring junior engineers so that when they come to you stuck, they leave sharper and more capable — not more dependent Communicating clearly with clients and vendors — technical enough to be credible, human enough to build trust Documenting everything you touch so clearly that others can build on it without asking you a single question What We’re Looking For 5+ years of IT experience, minimum 2 in an MSP or multi-client environment — you’ve seen enough environments to know what good looks like Deep proficiency in Windows Server (2016–2022), Active Directory, DNS/DHCP, Group Policy, and virtualization (VMware/Hyper-V) — you don’t need to look up the basics Strong networking fundamentals — routing, switching, VLANs, firewall management (SonicWall, Fortinet, or Meraki); you can trace a problem through the stack Solid Microsoft 365 and Azure experience (AZ-104 level or equivalent) — you know the platform well enough to administer, troubleshoot, and advise clients on it PowerShell scripting proficiency — you write scripts that work, that others can read, and that solve real operational problems Hands-on experience with RMM and PSA platforms (Datto, HaloPSA, NinjaRMM, or equivalent) — you know how to use your tools, not just log into them VOIP support experience (3CX or similar) — you can configure, troubleshoot, and advise without escalating Strong diagnostic skills — you find root causes, not just the closest available fix Natural mentor — you make the engineers around you better without making them dependent on you Self-directed once you understand the environment — but you ask smart questions early to get there Honest communicator — you say what’s happening clearly and on time, even when the update isn’t good news Long-term mindset — you want to grow with a company, not job-hop How Onboarding Works (And How That Changes) We’re honest about how we work with new team members. In your first weeks and months, you’ll have real hands-on support from the team. We’ll work closely with you to understand client environments, our standards, our tools, and how we operate. We expect questions — and we’ll give you the time to learn. As you show what you can do — and you will — that involvement steps back quickly. Trust grows fast here. People who demonstrate ownership, judgment, and quality work earn autonomy quickly. People who consistently deliver end up with significant latitude to shape their work, their tools, and their clients’ environments. We don’t want to over-direct people who don’t need it. But we also don’t believe in throwing someone in without context and calling it autonomy. We earn each other’s trust through real work — then we get out of the way. What Success Looks Like At 3 months: You’ve taken full ownership of your client environments Escalations from junior engineers are getting cleaner answers — and fewer of them You know where the gaps are and you’ve already started closing them At 6 months: Recurring issues in your client environments are measurably down — because you automated them away, not just resolved them faster Your documentation and scripts are becoming what the team reaches for first At 12 months: Clients notice the quality difference The “this issue again?” moments have dropped significantly Junior engineers are visibly sharper from working with you You’ve shaped how we operate — and the business is investing in your next step How We Work We trust our people to do great work and we give them the space to do it. There’s real work to be done — clients depend on us — but we understand that doing meaningful work means making decisions, and sometimes those decisions won’t be right on the first try. That’s how we find the right path. We don’t punish thoughtful mistakes. We learn from them and move forward. We’re a growing MSP, so not every system is perfect yet and not every process is documented. That’s part of why we need someone like you — to help us build the automations, processes, and systems that make us better as we grow. This Role Is Not for You If: You need step-by-step direction to know what to do next You fix the symptom and move on without asking why You treat documentation as someone else’s responsibility You’re comfortable letting a ticket sit without following through Your outside commitments regularly affect your availability and focus You’re looking for a stable, low-pressure remote job with a predictable routine There’s no judgment in that list — it’s just not the environment. We’d rather be honest now than waste each other’s time. What We Offer Competitive salary based on experience 100% remote — no corporate commute, ever Weekly pay via Wise direct deposit Tech and internet allowance Paid vacation leave HMO Access to the latest AI tools (Claude, Cursor, and more) to work smarter and automate more Career growth opportunities in a fast-moving, systems-focused environment

Full job description

**PLEASE CAREFULLY READ ALL THE DETAILS BEFORE APPLYING*** Job Title: AI & Automation Engineer Rate: TBD Work Type: Remote, Full-time Working Hours: Mon–Fri: 8am – 5pm CST Start Date: TBD JOB OVERVIEW: The AI & Automation Engineer is responsible for the strategic design, development, and operational management of automation solutions, data pipelines, and AI-powered integrations. This role drives operational efficiency and business intelligence by leveraging modern tools including MCP servers, Claude AI, and Microsoft Copilot to deliver scalable, intelligent automation. The AI & Automation Engineer fosters a culture of innovation, accountability, and continuous improvement, while ensuring compliance with company policies and industry best practices. JOB ROLE & RESPONSIBILITIES: Strategic Leadership -Define and execute the strategic roadmap for automation and data engineering in alignment with company goals. -Partner with business and IT leadership to develop scalable automation and data solutions. -Monitor industry trends and incorporate best practices to maintain competitive and efficient operations. Solution Delivery & Operations -Design, build, and maintain automation workflows to streamline IT and business processes. -Develop, test, and deploy data pipelines for ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) operations. -Integrate automation and data solutions with existing systems and applications. -Design, deploy, and maintain MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers to extend AI capabilities and integrate Claude-powered tooling into business workflows. -Leverage Claude AI and large language model capabilities to automate analysis, generate insights, and accelerate solution development. -Monitor and optimize the performance of automation scripts and data flows. -Ensure high availability and reliability of automation jobs and data pipelines. -Maintain process documentation and contribute to knowledge base articles. -Ensure compliance with security, regulatory, and industry best practices. Team Leadership & Development -Collaborate with cross-functional teams, providing technical guidance and mentorship. -Share knowledge and best practices with team members. -Support professional development and training initiatives related to automation and data engineering, and AI integration practices. Stakeholder Relationship Management -Work closely with business stakeholders, data analysts, and IT teams to gather requirements and deliver solutions. -Communicate effectively with technical and non-technical stakeholders. -Support project planning, status reporting, and solution presentations. Process & Continuous Improvement -Identify and implement improvements to operational workflows, automation frameworks, and data engineering practices. -Leverage automation, documentation, and tools to increase efficiency and reduce manual effort. -Track work, time entry, and project progress accurately and promptly. -Stay current with emerging technologies in automation, data engineering, analytics, and AI/LLM tooling. JOB REQUIREMENTS: Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities -Proficiency in scripting languages (e.g., Python, PowerShell, Bash) and automation platforms (e.g., Power BI, Power Automate). -Experience with data engineering tools and frameworks (e.g., SQL, ETL tools, Azure Data Factory). -Experience with Azure Copilot Studio and GitHub Copilot; ability to configure, customize, and govern AI Copilot experiences across the Microsoft 365 ecosystem. -Hands-on experience designing and configuring MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers, including tool definitions, authentication, and integration with AI assistants such as Claude. -Strong understanding of database systems (SQL/NoSQL), data modeling, and data integration. -Familiarity with cloud platforms (e.g., Azure, AWS, GCP) and their automation/data services. -Strong analytical, problem-solving, and troubleshooting skills. -Ability to document technical solutions and communicate effectively. -Strong organizational and time management skills. -Ability to multitask, adapt to changes quickly, and work effectively in a team environment. Performance Metrics -Uptime and reliability of automation jobs and data pipelines. -Timeliness and quality of automation and data engineering deliverables. -SLA and KPI adherence for automation and data services. -Client and stakeholder satisfaction and feedback. -Contribution to process improvements and team knowledge sharing. -Adoption and effectiveness of AI/MCP integrations deployed for internal or client use.

Full job description

Deal & Executive Operations Associate (Remote) About the Role We are seeking a highly capable, resourceful, and intellectually curious Deal & Executive Operations Associate to serve as the right hand to an entrepreneur, investor, and acquisition executive. This is a unique hybrid role combining executive assistance, research, business development, investor support, and strategic operations. The ideal candidate enjoys learning new industries, researching companies, finding opportunities, building relationships, and helping an executive operate at a higher level. You will work directly with the Founder on acquisitions, investments, business development, executive administration, travel planning, networking, and special projects. This is not a traditional administrative assistant position. We are looking for a Swiss Army knife who can think independently, solve problems, and execute with minimal oversight. Key Responsibilities M&A and Investment Research Research industries, markets, and investment themes Identify acquisition targets and attractive companies Build prospect lists and market maps Analyze company websites, financial information, and business models Prepare concise investment summaries and briefing materials Summarize industry reports, articles, podcasts, and research Monitor sectors of interest and surface new opportunities Business Development & Outreach Develop lists of CEOs, founders, owners, and executives Conduct outreach through email, LinkedIn, phone calls, and other channels Schedule introductory meetings Track outreach campaigns and follow-up activity Maintain CRM records and relationship notes Help expand professional networks and sourcing channels Executive Support Manage email inbox and prioritize communications Draft email responses and correspondence Coordinate calendar and scheduling Prepare meeting agendas and follow-up notes Track action items and ensure completion Organize files, documents, and projects Research & Intelligence Conduct deep-dive research on people, companies, industries, and trends Monitor competitors and market developments Identify conferences, events, and networking opportunities Prepare briefing books before meetings Find relevant contacts and relationship pathways Personal Operations Research and coordinate travel Build travel itineraries Assist with reservations and logistics Research family travel, schools, activities, and personal projects Help manage personal administrative tasks LinkedIn & Relationship Management Maintain and optimize LinkedIn presence Identify interesting executives, founders, investors, and operators Recommend networking opportunities Draft LinkedIn messages and outreach campaigns Track important relationships and follow-ups Special Projects Solve ad hoc business and personal problems Create presentations, spreadsheets, and reports Assist with recruiting efforts Coordinate vendors and service providers Support portfolio company initiatives when needed What Success Looks Like After 12 months, you will: Consistently keep the executive organized and focused Surface acquisition and investment opportunities proactively Build and maintain outreach pipelines Prepare concise, actionable research summaries Ensure meetings, travel, and logistics run smoothly Become a trusted thought partner capable of operating independently Ideal Candidate You may be a fit if you: Love learning about businesses and industries Enjoy research and finding answers Are naturally curious and resourceful Write exceptionally well Are comfortable reaching out to senior executives Can manage many projects simultaneously Take ownership without being asked Have excellent judgment and discretion Enjoy both strategic work and detailed execution Preferred Qualifications 3–8 years of experience in executive assistance, consulting, private equity, investment banking, executive recruiting, business development, venture capital, or startup operations Strong written and verbal English communication skills Advanced proficiency in Google Workspace and Microsoft Office Experience with CRM systems (HubSpot, Salesforce, Pipedrive, etc.) Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator Experience using AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Perplexity, and automation platforms Ability to synthesize complex information into concise summaries Traits We Value Most Intellectual curiosity Resourcefulness Responsiveness Strong writing ability Organization Follow-through Professionalism Attention to detail Comfort with ambiguity Bias toward action Compensation Remote position Flexible working hours with overlap during U.S. business hours Competitive salary based on experience

Full job description

We are looking for a highly organized, systems-driven Executive Operations Manager to work directly with the CEO of a fast-growing digital marketing and AI solutions agency. This role is part executive assistant, part operations manager, and part systems architect. You will be responsible for organizing the business, building and maintaining SOPs, managing communications, and ensuring nothing falls through the cracks. If you thrive in fast-paced environments and love turning chaos into scalable systems, this role is for you. Key Responsibilities Manage all executive communications, including email, Slack, client messages, and internal updates; ensure timely responses and zero missed opportunities. Build, document, and maintain SOPs across all departments (marketing, development, sales, client fulfillment). Organize business operations inside tools like ClickUp, CRMs, and automation platforms (n8n or similar). Act as the CEO’s right hand, prioritizing tasks, managing schedules, and ensuring execution on high-impact initiatives. Identify inefficiencies and implement systems to improve workflows and team productivity. Coordinate between departments, contractors, and clients to ensure alignment and accountability. Track key metrics, deadlines, and deliverables across multiple projects. Assist in hiring, onboarding, and process standardization for new team members. Create internal documentation, training materials, and process maps. Ideal Candidate Extremely organized and detail-oriented with strong follow-through. Experienced in building SOPs and operational systems from scratch. Comfortable managing multiple communication channels without missing details. Strong understanding of digital marketing, SaaS, or agency environments. Tech-savvy with experience in tools like ClickUp, Slack, CRMs, Google Workspace, and automation tools. Able to think proactively, solve problems independently, and anticipate needs. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Comfortable working directly with a CEO and handling sensitive information. Bonus Skills (Highly Preferred) Experience with AI tools and automation (ChatGPT, n8n, Zapier, etc.). Background in digital marketing agencies or SaaS companies. Familiarity with project management frameworks and workflow optimization. Experience managing remote teams and contractors. What Success Looks Like The CEO has a clear, organized workflow with no missed messages or tasks. SOPs are documented, updated, and actively used by the team. Projects move forward without bottlenecks or confusion. Communication across the business is streamlined and efficient. Systems are in place that allow the business to scale without chaos.

Full job description

We're looking for a highly organized and proactive Instagram Operations Lead to oversee our Instagram team and ensure smooth day-to-day operations. Responsibilities: Act as the primary point of contact between the CEO and the Instagram department (the CEO communicates directly only with the IG Lead). Supervise and support the Instagram team, including posters, editors, and content producers, while helping onboard future team members. Review and evaluate content to determine whether it meets Instagram guidelines and quality standards. Troubleshoot operational issues involving automation tools, Doritos/container setup, remote access software, and other internal systems. Research trends and gather reference materials for the Instagram Content Producer to use in Airtable workflows. Help improve team processes and ensure projects are completed efficiently. Requirements: Willing to work overtime and adjust your schedule when needed. Previous leadership or team management experience (experience managing 5+ people is a plus). Strong written and spoken English communication skills. Based in or near Manila is an advantage. Fast learner with excellent problem-solving and organizational skills. Experience with phone farms, AI tools (such as Higgsfield.ai and Nano Banana), and Cursor is a plus but not required. Schedule: Monday-Saturday, 8:00 AM-4:00 PM PHT (8 hours/day) Compensation: $3/hour Apply here: airtable.com/appHV16FVRHwVrnPV/shrsX4Qgv5L8ppaLy

Full job description

To fast-track your application, please fill out the form below: emfhr.netlify.app/f/37c82e66-2517-81ba-95fb-d084504e89a5?source=OnlineJobps.ph&role=37c82e66-2517-81f7-9144-d477572475a7 About the Role We are seeking a highly capable Executive Assistant to support our Chief Financial Officer (CFO). This is not a traditional administrative role. We are looking for someone who combines strong executive support capabilities with a solid financial background, particularly in payroll, financial administration, and operational finance. You will act as a trusted extension of the CFO, helping manage critical financial processes and maintaining accuracy across payroll and reporting workflows. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, learns quickly, and can confidently navigate both executive-level responsibilities and financial operations. We are looking for an individual with strong maths skills, highly organised, exceptional attention to detail, and a proven track record of taking ownership and solving problems independently. What You Will Be Doing Executive Support to the CFO Prepare reports, summaries, presentations, and executive documentation Track critical deadlines and ensure timely execution of financial and operational initiatives Liaise with internal departments and external stakeholders on behalf of the CFO Payroll & Financial Administration Assist with payroll processing and payroll-related administration Follow up with customers regarding payments Review payroll data for completeness, accuracy, and compliance Coordinate with HR and Finance teams to ensure employee records and compensation data remain up-to-date Support the reconciliation of payroll-related transactions Assist with contractor payments, commission tracking, and other compensation-related processes Track software license usage on a monthly basis to ensure that those no longer in use are cancelled Maintain confidential financial and employee information with the highest level of discretion Financial Operations & Reporting Support the preparation of financial reports and operational dashboards Assist with budget tracking, expense monitoring, and financial documentation Organize and maintain financial records and supporting documentation Conduct research and analysis to support business decisions Help identify process improvement opportunities within finance and operations Process Improvement & Project Coordination Create structure and documentation around financial workflows Help streamline processes through automation and AI tools Coordinate special projects led by the CFO Proactively identify inefficiencies and recommend practical solutions Who We Are Looking For Required Qualifications Minimum 2 years of professional experience in Executive Assistant, Financial Administrator, Payroll Administrator, Finance Coordinator, or similar roles Strong experience with payroll administration and payroll processes Experience supporting senior leaders, executives, or finance leadership teams Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets Excellent written and verbal English communication skills Preferred Qualifications Experience supporting a CFO, Finance Director, or senior finance executive Background in accounting, finance, payroll, bookkeeping, or financial operations Experience with payroll systems and HRIS platforms Exposure to budgeting, reconciliations, reporting, or financial analysis Experience in remote, startup, tech, or high-growth business environments Core Competencies High Cognitive Ability We are looking for someone who demonstrates: Strong analytical thinking Excellent judgment and decision-making Ability to learn new systems and processes quickly Strong problem-solving capabilities Ability to connect information across multiple business functions Ownership Mentality Takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks Proactively follows up until issues are fully resolved Requires minimal supervision once objectives are understood Anticipates needs before they become problems Exceptional Attention to Detail Maintains a high level of accuracy in payroll, financial records, and reporting Consistently catches discrepancies and potential errors Understands the importance of precision when handling financial information Organization & Prioritization Able to manage multiple competing priorities simultaneously Creates structure in ambiguous situations Maintains clear systems and documentation Technology & Tools You should be comfortable working with: Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets Google Workspace Payroll and HR systems Project management tools (Notion, Airtable, ClickUp, Asana, or similar) AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini Practical Requirements Reliable computer and high-speed internet connection Dedicated home office or quiet work environment Ability to work within overlapping hours with international teams Comfortable handling confidential financial and payroll information Why This Role This is a high-trust role working directly with the CFO and supporting key financial operations of the business. You will gain exposure to executive decision-making, financial strategy, and operational leadership while playing a meaningful role in helping the company scale. We are looking for someone who is intelligent, proactive, highly organized, and eager to grow. If you're the type of person who learns quickly, takes ownership naturally, and enjoys solving complex problems, we'd love to hear from you. Work hours: 10AM-6PM Philippine time zone To FAST TRACK your application, please apply on the link below: emfhr.netlify.app/f/37c82e66-2517-81ba-95fb-d084504e89a5?source=OnlineJobps.ph&jobPost=37c82e66-2517-8190-a3a4-c237741daf53&role=37c82e66-2517-81f7-9144-d477572475a7

Please apply on the link below: valiant-bovid-3df.notion.site/93382e662517820c95e78173ebbcd53d?pvs=105 About the Role We are seeking a highly capable Executive Assistant to support our Chief Financial Officer (CFO). This is not a traditional administrative role. We are looking for someone who combines strong executive support capabilities with a solid financial background, particularly in payroll, financial administration, and operational finance. You will act as a trusted extension of the CFO, helping manage critical financial processes and maintaining accuracy across payroll and reporting workflows. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments, learns quickly, and can confidently navigate both executive-level responsibilities and financial operations. We are looking for an individual with strong maths skills, highly organised, exceptional attention to detail, and a proven track record of taking ownership and solving problems independently. What You Will Be Doing - Executive Support to the CFO - Prepare reports, summaries, presentations, and executive documentation - Track critical deadlines and ensure timely execution of financial and operational initiatives - Liaise with internal departments and external stakeholders on behalf of the CFO Payroll & Financial Administration - Assist with payroll processing and payroll-related administration - Follow up with customers regarding payments - Review payroll data for completeness, accuracy, and compliance - Coordinate with HR and Finance teams to ensure employee records and compensation data remain up-to-date - Support the reconciliation of payroll-related transactions - Assist with contractor payments, commission tracking, and other compensation-related processes - Track software license usage on a monthly basis to ensure that those no longer in use are cancelled - Maintain confidential financial and employee information with the highest level of discretion Financial Operations & Reporting - Support the preparation of financial reports and operational dashboards - Assist with budget tracking, expense monitoring, and financial documentation - Organize and maintain financial records and supporting documentation - Conduct research and analysis to support business decisions - Help identify process improvement opportunities within finance and operations - Process Improvement & Project Coordination - Create structure and documentation around financial workflows - Help streamline processes through automation and AI tools - Coordinate special projects led by the CFO - Proactively identify inefficiencies and recommend practical solutions Who We Are Looking For Required Qualifications - Minimum 2 years of professional experience in Executive Assistant, Financial Administrator, Payroll Administrator, Finance Coordinator, or similar roles - Strong experience with payroll administration and payroll processes - Experience supporting senior leaders, executives, or finance leadership teams - Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets - Excellent written and verbal English communication skills Preferred Qualifications - Experience supporting a CFO, Finance Director, or senior finance executive - Background in accounting, finance, payroll, bookkeeping, or financial operations - Experience with payroll systems and HRIS platforms - Exposure to budgeting, reconciliations, reporting, or financial analysis - Experience in remote, startup, tech, or high-growth business environments Core Competencies - High Cognitive Ability - We are looking for someone who demonstrates: - Strong analytical thinking - Excellent judgment and decision-making - Ability to learn new systems and processes quickly - Strong problem-solving capabilities - Ability to connect information across multiple business functions Ownership Mentality - Takes responsibility for outcomes, not just tasks - Proactively follows up until issues are fully resolved - Requires minimal supervision once objectives are understood - Anticipates needs before they become problems Exceptional Attention to Detail - Maintains a high level of accuracy in payroll, financial records, and reporting - Consistently catches discrepancies and potential errors - Understands the importance of precision when handling financial information Organization & Prioritization - Able to manage multiple competing priorities simultaneously - Creates structure in ambiguous situations - Maintains clear systems and documentation Technology & Tools - You should be comfortable working with: - Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets - Google Workspace - Payroll and HR systems - Project management tools (Notion, Airtable, ClickUp, Asana, or similar) - AI tools such as ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini Practical Requirements - Reliable computer and high-speed internet connection - Dedicated home office or quiet work environment - Ability to work within overlapping hours with international teams - Comfortable handling confidential financial and payroll information Why This Role This is a high-trust role working directly with the CFO and supporting key financial operations of the business. You will gain exposure to executive decision-making, financial strategy, and operational leadership while playing a meaningful role in helping the company scale. We are looking for someone who is intelligent, proactive, highly organized, and eager to grow. If you're the type of person who learns quickly, takes ownership naturally, and enjoys solving complex problems, we'd love to hear from you. Work hours: 10AM-6PM Philippine time zone

Full job description

We’re looking for a reliable Funnel Builder to join our ecommerce team full-time. Not just for a few weeks. Not just for random projects. We’re building a real long-term operation with people that want stability, growth, and a strong team around them. A lot of our best people started in smaller roles and worked themselves up over time through consistency, quality, and reliability. What You’ll Actually Do You will build and localize ecommerce funnels for different European markets like: • Sweden • Germany • Netherlands / Belgium • Israel • Poland • Norway You’ll work inside: • Funnelish • Shopify • Wetracked integrations • Our internal systems and templates This is NOT about designing from scratch all day. It’s about: • following proven structures • adapting funnels correctly, understanding what and when to add things to our template • Work with Snapping tool and our Claude.ai html output machine • Work with gemini, chatgpt pro and magnific on image generations instead of manual editing • editing graphics/images when needed • making sure names, prices, bundles, offers and layouts are perfect • understanding how the backend connects together Attention to detail matters a lot here. You Don’t Need To Know Everything We will teach: • better AI workflows • image upscaling/editing methods • localization systems • optimization tricks • our funnel standards But you MUST: • learn fast • care about quality • communicate clearly • double-check your work Team Culture We are not a freelancer marketplace. We have: • departments • team structure • support systems • weekly calls • long-term growth opportunities We want people who enjoy being part of a team and improving over time. Compensation & Growth • Starting pay: around $5/hour • Strong performers can grow to $6–6.5/hour+ • Paid sprint challenges • Bonus opportunities • Stable full-time workload • Long-term security for reliable team members For many people in our team, this became a stable long-term income source. We’re Probably A Good Fit If You: • already built funnels before, undertsand html a bit, you rarely do mistakes and adjust fully to our workflows. • understand Shopify basics • know how to work carefully • can follow templates and systems • enjoy structured work • want stable long-term work instead of random clients To Apply Send: Funnel examples you built (latest links) Your experience with Funnelish / Shopify AI tools you use Your average funnel build time Why you want a long-term role We care much more about reliability and consistency than ego. If your work is clean and you’re dependable, you’ll fit in well here. Thank you on this matter, send Discord Name and Whatsapp Number for faster chances of employment

You want to get real-world experience with automation systems and building AI-driven workflows. 3. Are you open to a long-term collaboration if we are a great fit for each other?

Full job description

Applicants without prior real estate industry experience will not be considered. Position Overview As our Executive Assistant & Content Manager, you'll play a key role in keeping both client deliverables and internal operations running smoothly. This role combines executive support, project coordination, content management, and digital marketing execution within the real estate industry. You'll work closely with leadership to ensure projects move forward efficiently while maintaining a high standard of quality. Because many of our clients are real estate professionals, we are looking for someone who already understands the industry and can confidently support agents, brokerages, and real estate-related marketing initiatives. Responsibilities Real Estate Marketing Support - Understand real estate terminology, transaction timelines, and industry workflows. - Assist with creating and reviewing real estate marketing content. - Support agent and brokerage clients with content needs and campaign execution. - Coordinate time-sensitive deliverables tied to listings, closings, events, and launches. - Ensure content aligns with real estate compliance standards and brand guidelines when applicable. Requirements Must-Have Experience - At least 2 years of experience as an Executive Assistant, Project Coordinator, Account Manager, or Digital Marketing VA. - Previous experience working with real estate agents, brokerages, real estate teams, or real estate marketing agencies. - Strong understanding of the real estate industry, including common terminology and agent workflows. - Excellent written and verbal English communication skills. - Strong organizational and time management abilities. - Experience managing multiple projects simultaneously. - High attention to detail. - Ability to work independently and solve problems proactively. Preferred Experience Experience with some or all of the following: - Canva - Descript - Metricool - Flodesk - ManyChat - Google Workspace - Project management tools [Asana, ClickUp, etc.] - Website platforms [WordPress, Lovable, Showit, Squarespace, or similar] - Social media reporting and analytics - Experience creating content specifically for real estate agents and brokerages - Familiarity with MLS, IDX websites, listing marketing, open house promotions, and agent branding What We're Looking For You are someone who: - Notices details others miss. - Takes ownership instead of waiting for instructions. - Communicates clearly and professionally. - Loves creating systems and improving processes. - Can shift between creative and administrative tasks with ease. - Understands the fast-paced nature of the real estate industry and can adapt quickly to changing priorities. - Enjoys being the person who keeps everything moving behind the scenes. How to Apply To help us identify candidates who pay attention to details, please begin your application with the phrase: "Details create excellence." In your application, please also answer the following: - Describe your experience working in the real estate industry. - What types of real estate professionals have you supported [agents, teams, brokerages, investors, etc.]? - Which real estate marketing tasks have you handled directly? - Which of the following tools have you used: Canva, Metricool, Flodesk, ManyChat, Asana, ClickUp, WordPress, Showit, Lovable? - What is your expected monthly salary?

Full job description

Role purpose We are looking for an AI-native digital growth specialist to help us grow organic search visibility, AI visibility, Google advertising performance and online marketing ROI. We are a trade-only B2B ecommerce supplier in Australia. We currently sell through a WooCommerce website and are moving to Shopify Plus in the medium term. This role will help manage and improve our search, content, advertising and reporting systems so our online marketing becomes more effective, more measurable and less dependent on external agencies. This is not just a traditional SEO role. We want someone who understands SEO, AEO, AI search visibility, Google Ads, content, analytics, ecommerce conversion and how to use AI properly to work faster and smarter. Core responsibilities 1. SEO and organic growth • Improve rankings for commercial B2B search terms, especially keywords moving into stronger ranking positions • Improve category pages, product pages, content pages, FAQs, internal links, metadata and headings • Use Google Search Console, GA4 and SEO tools to identify opportunities and track results • Support technical SEO work including redirects, canonicals, schema, indexation, crawl issues and ranking-sensitive page structure • Support the WooCommerce to Shopify Plus migration from an SEO preservation and growth perspective 2. AEO, AI visibility and AI-native workflows • Improve visibility in AI-generated answers and AI-assisted search journeys • Track visibility across tools and environments such as Google AI Overviews, Gemini, ChatGPT, Perplexity, Copilot and Bing where practical • Use AI tools properly for keyword clustering, competitor research, content briefs, ad copy testing, reporting, content improvement and workflow automation • Create repeatable AI-assisted processes without compromising quality, accuracy or brand trust • Keep moving with how buyers are using AI to research products and suppliers 3. Google Ads and performance marketing • Be fully conversant with Google advertising products, including Search Ads, Shopping, Performance Max, YouTube, Demand Gen, Display, remarketing, Merchant Centre, GA4 and Google Tag Manager • Help improve paid search and paid marketing ROI • Review campaign structure, search terms, landing pages, conversion tracking and performance reporting 4. Content, authority and outreach • Build content plans around real trade buyer needs, not generic blog activity • Improve domain authority / reputation consistently • Improve existing pages and create content briefs for new pages, guides, FAQs and support content • Help build authority through relevant supplier, manufacturer, trade, partner, industry and content opportunities • Focus on quality backlinks, genuine mentions and commercially useful authority wins rather than low-value link volume • Track backlinks, referring domains, target pages, anchor text, quality, relevance and live status 5. Reporting and commercial accountability • Build and maintain clear reporting across SEO, AEO, Google Ads and online marketing performance • Report on organic traffic, keyword movement, ranking improvements, organic orders, new account signups, enquiries and revenue where available • Report on AI visibility and brand mentions in AI/search environments • Track marketing activity against commercial outcomes, not just activity completed • Identify what is working, what is not working, and what should change Key performance expectations The role will be measured against agreed internal baselines and commercial outcomes, including: • Improving our overall domain authority, domain rating and search authority over time • Increasing organic traffic from priority commercial search terms • Increasing the number of priority keywords ranking in stronger positions • Improving measurable AI visibility month by month • Improving ROI from Google Ads and other digital marketing channels used • Creating clear monthly reporting that links activity to sales, enquiries, new accounts, traffic, rankings and commercial impact • Maintaining safe, sustainable and quality-focused SEO practices Required skills and experience • Strong practical SEO experience, preferably in ecommerce or B2B ecommerce • Experience improving category pages, product pages and commercial landing pages • Strong Google Search Console, GA4 and keyword tracking experience • Strong working knowledge of Google Ads, including Search, Shopping, Performance Max and remarketing • Strong understanding of content-led SEO and authority building • Practical understanding of AI search, AEO and how AI tools are changing search behaviour • Confident using AI tools in day-to-day work to improve speed, quality and output • Able to build reports that management can understand and act on • Able to work remotely with clear priorities, accountability and regular updates • Able to challenge weak assumptions and give practical recommendations, not just agree with everything Nice-to-haves • Shopify Plus SEO experience • WooCommerce SEO experience • Experience with B2B, trade, industrial, ecommerce or wholesale businesses • Experience with Semrush, Ahrefs, SE Ranking, Screaming Frog, Looker Studio or similar tools • Experience with Google Merchant Centre and product feed optimisation • Experience with Microsoft/Bing Webmaster Tools and AI visibility tracking • Basic HTML/CSS understanding • Experience working with developers on technical SEO tasks • Experience creating content briefs for writers, designers or internal team members Tools and workflow • Work managed through a simple task board such as Basecamp or similar • Weekly priorities agreed with management • Clear monthly reporting required • Standard process for SEO tasks, content briefs, ad reviews, technical requests and reporting • All key tools, accounts and data must remain under company ownership and access Employment model • Part-time or full time role • Long-term role for the right person We are not looking for someone who only produces generic SEO activity. We want someone who can help us build a stronger internal digital growth system across SEO, AI visibility, Google Ads, content, reporting and ecommerce conversion. The right person will be commercially minded, practical, direct, organised and outcome-focused. Checklist for your application Please include the following in your application: 1. Your experience with ecommerce SEO, especially category pages and product pages 2. Your experience with Google Ads, including which Google Ads products you have managed 3. Your experience with AI tools and how you use them in real marketing workflows 4. Your understanding of AEO / AI visibility and how you would track it 5. Your experience with Shopify, Shopify Plus or WooCommerce SEO 6. The SEO tools and reporting tools you have used 7. Examples of ranking, traffic, revenue, lead or ROI improvements you have helped achieve 8. One example of how you would improve a B2B ecommerce category page 9. One example of how you would assess backlink or authority quality 10. A short explanation of how you would approach the first 90 days in this role Please do not apply if your SEO approach is mainly based on low-quality backlinks, generic blog posting, directory submissions or activity reports that do not connect to commercial outcomes.

Full job description

Elite Digital Operations Engineer & Executive Technology Specialist (Remote) About Us We are building a fast-growing business that serves executives and companies by creating world-class digital systems, websites, automation, branding, marketing assets, and operational infrastructure. We are not looking for an average virtual assistant. We are looking for an exceptional digital professional who enjoys building businesses, solving complex problems, and producing high-quality work across multiple disciplines. If you're someone who can take an idea from a conversation and turn it into a polished, scalable solution, we want to hear from you. --- Responsibilities You should be highly proficient in several of the following areas: - Website design and development (front-end and back-end) - AI implementation and workflow automation - Graphic design and branding - UI/UX design - Professional document creation - Business presentations and proposals - CRM setup and management - Process documentation - Video editing and motion graphics - Content creation and social media management - Search engine optimization (SEO) - Email marketing - Business systems engineering - Project management - Research and data organization - Cloud-based collaboration tools - Troubleshooting technical issues independently We value professionals who don't simply complete tasks—they improve systems, identify opportunities, and recommend better solutions. --- Minimum Qualifications You should: - Have at least 5 years of professional digital experience. - Have advanced English communication skills. - Be available during agreed working hours. - Have reliable high-speed internet and a professional workspace. - Be highly organized and dependable. - Be proactive and solution-oriented. - Be comfortable learning new software quickly. - Maintain strict confidentiality with company information. --- Required Software Experience Please indicate your experience level with each of the following: - WordPress - Webflow - Shopify - Figma - Adobe Photoshop - Adobe Illustrator - Adobe Premiere Pro - Canva - Microsoft Office - Google Workspace - ChatGPT and other AI tools - Notion - Trello - ClickUp - Monday.com - HubSpot - Salesforce - Zapier - Make.com - HTML - CSS - JavaScript - PHP - Python - SQL --- Required Application Submission To be considered, submit the following: 1. Your résumé. 2. A portfolio of your best work. 3. Links to live websites you personally built. 4. Three examples of business documents or presentations you created. 5. Three examples of graphic design or branding projects. 6. One automation or AI workflow you designed and how it improved efficiency. 7. A 2–3 minute video introducing yourself, explaining your experience, and describing the project you are most proud of. 8. The names of every software platform you are genuinely proficient in. Please do not list software you have only used briefly. --- Skills Assessment Shortlisted candidates will complete a paid practical assessment. The assessment may include: - Designing a landing page - Editing graphics - Building a professional business proposal - Creating a workflow automation - Organizing a CRM - Improving an existing website - Solving a technical problem independently We hire based on demonstrated ability—not résumé claims. --- Our Ideal Candidate We're looking for someone who: - Thinks like an entrepreneur. - Pays attention to every detail. - Can work with minimal supervision. - Learns quickly. - Takes ownership of projects. - Communicates clearly and professionally. - Delivers work on time. - Consistently exceeds expectations. If you enjoy creating exceptional work and helping businesses grow through technology, we'd love to meet you. --- Important Instructions To confirm that you've read this posting in full, begin your application with the phrase: "Digital Excellence Begins Here." Applications that do not include this phrase or fail to provide the requested materials may not be considered. We value honesty, professionalism, and excellence. If your work consistently demonstrates those qualities, you'll have the opportunity to grow with our company for years to come.

Executive Assistant to the Founder

Good work should win the work. Rootl helps tradespeople and local service businesses get found, picked and booked online. We exist because good businesses lose to worse ones every day. Not because they are worse at the job, but because the worse ones got...

claudechatgptai toolsperplexityrepoapiautomationautomationsghl
Why it belongs here
  • AI/build signals: claude, chatgpt, ai tools, perplexity

Arca angle

Full job description

Good work should win the work. Rootl helps tradespeople and local service businesses get found, picked and booked online. We exist because good businesses lose to worse ones every day. Not because they are worse at the job, but because the worse ones got found first. It started with advice from an upstairs window to a window cleaner halfway up a ladder. By 2036 we want Rootl to be the UK's most trusted local search company for trades and local service businesses, with our first US hub open. We are building the team that gets us there. Want a seat on the bus? ## WHAT WE DO We help tradespeople and local service businesses get found on Google, and inside the AI tools their next customer is already searching with: ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity, Gemini. That is the job. We do it for plumbers, electricians, roofers, joiners, waste firms, architects, developers, and the trades around them. Three core services, one job done properly: getting our clients found when their next customer is looking. We are a small, growing team. The founder runs sales and strategy. A web developer builds the client sites. A content operations manager runs the content. This new seat is the founder's right hand, the person who holds everything together so the rest of us can do our jobs. ## THE ROLE, IN ONE LINE You protect the founder's time and turn what is in his head into a system the business can run on. ## ABOUT THIS SEAT This is the founder's right hand. The person who holds the structure, protects the time, and takes the mental load so the founder can focus on growing the business. ## READ THIS TWICE The next part is the honest version. Read it carefully, because it tells you exactly what you would be walking into. ## A STRAIGHT WORD, FOUNDER TO YOU I have never hired someone into this seat before. For two years I have run this business out of my own head. The inbox, the calendar, the onboarding, the follow-ups, the systems, all of it lives in my memory and on my plate. It has got me this far. It will not get me where we are going. I know that to scale this business I have to get the mentalness out of my head and into a system someone else can run. That is the job. You are not walking into a finished machine. You are helping me build it, then helping me let go of it. So be clear on what you are walking into. I move fast. I set twelve things going in a week and I need someone to catch the three that fell. Some weeks will feel like organised chaos. This role exists to bring order to it. If that excites you rather than worries you, this is your seat. It is a rocket ship, and I want you on it. ## WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE Here is the real test, and it comes from my corporate days before Rootl. I had an emergency appendectomy three days before a major event in San Diego that I was running. I could not fly. So I wrote up a full handover plan and passed it to my boss from the back of the car while my replacement was on their way to the airport. The event ran without me, because everything they needed was written down. That is what winning looks like in this seat. If I take an afternoon off, or a week off, the system runs itself. You are the person who makes that true, by turning what is in my head into something written down that anyone can pick up. There is a second, quieter version of success, and it happens every single day. You become the gateway. I have taken email off my phone and handed the inbox to you. You manage my calendar, not me. I do not always know what is coming up next week, because I do not need to. You do. I trust the surface you show me to be right, and that trust is the whole point of the seat. ## TRUST AND CONFIDENTIALITY This is the part that matters most, so read it carefully. This seat sees things almost no one else does. My inbox, my calendar, the numbers, the decisions, the conversations I would not have anywhere else. The role only works on a foundation of complete trust. We need someone with a high degree of integrity, discretion, and confidentiality. What you see in this seat stays in this seat. This is written into the contract as the first clause, and we will talk about it openly at interview, because it is not a detail. It is the ground the whole role stands on. ## WHY YOU'D WANT THIS SEAT - A real seat at the table. You work closely with the founder, you are the eyes and ears across the team, and you are someone I can bat ideas off. This is not a fetch-and-carry role. - A clear lane that is yours. I run sales, marketing and strategy. Our web developer builds the sites. Our content manager runs the content engine. You hold the operation together and protect my time. Everyone knows their seat, and this one is yours. - Responsibility from day one. Not a six-month shadow. - You help write the process, not just follow it. Using the founder's own recordings (camcorder method), voice notes, and you build the SOPs every future Rootl hire is trained on. - Variety with depth. You will do a range of things, and you will also do similar things well and make them tighter each time. - Real long-term upside for the person who stays the course. We will talk that through properly once you are established. - The local search world is shifting under everyone's feet right now. You will be close to the front of it. - We hire the right person, not the perfect person. Every candidate is DISC profiled, because the seat has to fit how we work. ## WHAT YOU WILL OWN - Inbox and calendar. You filter the inbox, surface only what I need, and protect my diary. I do not want email on my phone. That is your world. - Helping me plan the week. What is scheduled, what can be pushed, what matters most. You are the person I benchmark against so I feel organised. - Meeting prep and follow-ups. Getting me ready before a call, and making sure nothing drops after it. - Marketing and offers admin. Organising campaigns, drafting offers from my rough notes, and crafting my weekly Thursday networking pitch with other business owners so I can review and rehearse it. - Client onboarding, owned end to end. Intake form, welcome email, CRM setup, contract back-end, getting people access to systems. One repeatable process, run the same way every time. - Sales support. Scheduling the inbound enquiries and DMs that come from our campaigns, running first-pass research on a prospect against our fit benchmark and handing me a profile sheet before the call, and chasing proposals through to a yes or a clean no. - Team conduit. Keeping Slack current and Notion up to date, documenting decisions as they happen, and holding our weekly team rhythm with the rest of the team. - System building. Working with me to document one process every week, so the business stops living in my head. - Tool stand-up. Owning the setup when a new system comes in: project boards, pipelines, trackers, CRM updates. There will also be ad hoc tasks. This seat suits someone who enjoys variety and likes being the person who keeps an eye on how things are running across the team. ## HOW THIS SEAT THINKS This is the standard I am building toward, and it is how I want this seat held from day one. These five run underneath everything above. - Protect my time. You filter what comes in and decide what actually needs me. Most things do not. Guarding my time and attention is the first job, and everything else sits under it. - Prioritise revenue. When everything feels urgent, you sort by what makes money or saves money first. That is the tie-breaker. The task that protects a client or wins a job comes before the one that just shouts loudest. - Design the play. You come prepared and you think ahead. You see the thing coming before it lands on me, and you have already worked out the options. You plan the week, the meeting, and the follow-up before I have to ask. - Answer everyone well. Every message gets a clear, kind reply, even when the answer is no. How we treat people is how we are remembered, and you are often the first voice they hear from Rootl. - Keep the calendar clean and clear. Every entry has the context I need to walk into it cold. Who, what, why, and what I need to have ready. No naked invites, no guessing, no surprises. ## WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR - You love systems. You see a messy process and you want to make it clean and repeatable. - You are a strong planner. Detail is your thing. You enjoy mapping out how something will run before it runs. - You are analytical and you will push back when something does not add up. Not on the person, on the logic. I want someone who tells me when I am wrong. - You are calm in chaos. A founder still building the rail does not rattle you. You bring order without needing everything to be tidy first. - You are good with people and relationships. You become the eyes and ears of the team without anyone feeling watched. - You are comfortable with ambiguity. This seat is being built as it runs, and you are helping to build it. - You take pride in doing similar work to a high standard, week in, week out, and improving it as you go. - Excellent written English. We will test this. Nice to have, not essential: - A bit of technical appetite. Comfort with tools like Zapier, simple automations, or project planning software. - Experience supporting a founder or senior leader directly. - Experience editing AI-written content to a publish-ready standard. - A handy eye for social content. If you know your way around Canva and can keep a light hand on the quality of social posts going out to local groups, that is a real plus. We have someone producing the content, but having an in-house person who knows the business well enough to sense-check it is genuinely useful. - A systems-and-time way of thinking. If you have read books like Buy Back Your Time by Dan Martell, The E-Myth by Michael E. Gerber, or you naturally think about how to protect a founder's time and turn chaos into a repeatable system, that is a real plus. It means we already speak the same language. ## HOW THIS SEAT WORKS WITH THE FOUNDER I run hot and fast. I set a lot in motion and I need someone to hold the structure underneath it. I use the word "comfortable" when I am settling for less than I should, and I say "it's in my head" when I mean I have not written it down yet. I race to the destination before the scaffolding exists, which is sometimes my greatest strength and sometimes my biggest risk. Your job is the pause valve, not the brake. You do not slow me down. You make sure the path exists for me to run on. Come to me with goals and a plan, never empty-handed. Hit your deadlines. Hold closure on decisions, because leaving things hanging is the thing I find hardest. I am direct, I am honest, and I will tell you what is on my mind. I am looking for someone who can do the same. ## TOOLS YOU WILL USE Google Workspace, Notion, Slack, HighLevel, Toggl, and AI tools like ChatGPT and Claude. I am already teaching AI to write in my voice, so you will be editing and shaping output, not starting from a blank page. Experience with Zapier and automation tools are a bonus. Training and SOPs provided. You do not need every tool on day one, but you do need to learn fast. ## HOURS, PAY AND HOW IT WORKS - Part-time to start, around 10 hours a week, paid at $10 USD an hour. Hours grow toward 20 a week within the first 90 days as we find our rhythm and as I hand more over. - We start with a short unpaid test task so we can see how you work. If it goes well, you move onto a paid trial, paid for the hours you actually work. - Your hours and your rate both grow as you prove yourself and as the business grows. The aim is to build this into a senior, long-term seat for the right person. - If the work ever drops, we give you at least one week's written notice of any change to your hours. - Paid weekly for your first month, then monthly after that, by Wise. - You track your time in Toggl. Your tracked hours are what you invoice, so it stays clear and fair on both sides. - Every Monday we run a 30-minute team meeting using the LIONS sheet: last week, issues, opportunities, the next seven days, and successes. You fill yours in and come ready to run your part. - Most mornings we jump on a quick 15-minute call to set up the day, what matters, what is moving, what I need from you. A slightly longer one at the start of the week to plan it out. This daily contact is how the seat works, so you need to be someone who is happy with that rhythm. - I try to work UK hours, 7:00 to 4:30. At least half your hours each week need to fall inside that window so we can work together properly. For you in the Philippines that is roughly 2pm to 11:30pm, so an evening pattern fits well. - This is an independent contractor role on a proper written agreement covering confidentiality, client data, and how we pay you. You look after your own taxes in the Philippines, and you provide your own computer, reliable internet, and a quiet place to work. - Four weeks' notice on either side if it is ever not working out. ## THE HIRING PROCESS We want this to be clear and fair, so here is exactly how it runs. Everything up to the offer is unpaid, and the test task is the same for everyone. 1. You apply (opening with the screening phrase below). 2. We shortlist. 3. We send you a short test task with a clear deadline. 4. You complete and return it. 5. We score it and shortlist again. 6. You complete a DISC profile (a short questionnaire, so we understand how you work). 7. You have an interview with the founder. 8. If it is a yes on both sides, we make a provisional offer: a paid trial of one to two weeks, paid for the hours you work. 9. If the trial goes well, we move into a longer arrangement. ## HOW TO APPLY - Start your application with the phrase "Save me a seat" so we know you have read this far. - Tell us, in a few lines, about a time you took something chaotic off someone's plate and made it run smoothly. - Tell us about a system or process you built or improved, and what changed because of it. - Share any examples of executive support, admin, or operations work you are proud of. - The test task, when it comes, will be a real early-morning brain dump from the founder, for you to sort, prioritise, and turn into a clear plan. Unpaid, and the same for everyone. ## A LAST WORD We are not the biggest name out there, and we are not trying to be, (yet!) We are building something properly, and this seat is fundamental to getting it right. If you have read this far and something here lit you up, that is a good sign. Show us why it should be you. Want a seat on the bus?
