Full job description

Job Title: AI & Business Growth Specialist Location: Remote Job Type: Part-Time | Long-Term Starting Rate: $5.00/hour (Performance & Revenue Review After 90 Days) Overview We are seeking a highly skilled, systems-oriented professional with expertise in AI, automation, APIs, GoHighLevel (GHL), social media management, digital marketing, online marketplaces, and e-commerce operations. This role is responsible for designing, implementing, and managing the technology and marketing systems that power our growing digital business ecosystem. You will build intelligent workflows, optimize business operations, automate repetitive processes, manage social media platforms, and develop scalable systems that support customer acquisition, sales, and long-term business growth. We're looking for someone who enjoys solving complex problems, thinks in systems rather than tasks, embraces AI as a productivity multiplier, and is committed to delivering measurable business results. Key Responsibilities AI, Automation & Systems Development Design, build, and implement AI-powered workflows that improve operational efficiency across multiple businesses. Develop scalable business systems that integrate marketing, sales, customer service, operations, and internal processes. Create and maintain automation workflows using AI platforms, APIs, webhooks, Zapier, Make.com, GoHighLevel, and other integration tools. Build and optimize CRM systems in GoHighLevel, including: Pipelines Automations Lead nurturing Sales funnels Forms Calendars Customer journeys Integrate CRMs, e-commerce platforms, marketplaces, payment systems, communication tools, and other SaaS applications into a unified ecosystem. Design scalable system architecture that supports business expansion and operational efficiency. Analyze workflows to identify bottlenecks and replace manual processes with automation wherever possible. Troubleshoot automation issues and continuously improve system reliability, security, and performance. Maintain documentation, workflow diagrams, and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) for all systems and automations. Research, test, and implement emerging AI technologies that improve productivity, customer experience, and operational performance. Social Media Management & Digital Marketing Manage multiple social media accounts across different brands while maintaining consistent branding and messaging. Develop and execute social media strategies that support business growth, customer engagement, and lead generation. Create, edit, schedule, and publish high-quality content using AI-assisted content creation tools. Design and maintain monthly content calendars aligned with marketing campaigns and promotional initiatives. Monitor engagement, audience growth, reach, and campaign performance across all platforms. Analyze social media analytics and provide actionable recommendations to improve visibility, conversions, and ROI. Respond to comments, messages, and customer inquiries when required while maintaining a professional brand voice. Repurpose content across multiple platforms to maximize reach and efficiency. Automate content scheduling, publishing, customer engagement, lead capture, and follow-up sequences using GoHighLevel and other automation platforms. Stay current on platform updates, social media trends, AI marketing tools, and emerging digital marketing strategies. E-Commerce & Marketplace Operations Support the development, optimization, and management of e-commerce stores and marketplace operations. Assist with product listings, inventory workflows, promotional campaigns, and customer experience improvements. Build systems that streamline marketplace operations and improve operational efficiency. Integrate marketplace platforms with CRM, automation, inventory, and marketing systems. Identify opportunities to improve conversion rates, customer acquisition, and operational scalability through automation and AI. Business Strategy & Process Improvement Collaborate with leadership to translate business objectives into scalable technology and marketing solutions. Recommend AI, automation, and digital marketing strategies that improve productivity and revenue. Ensure accurate data synchronization and seamless information flow across all connected platforms. Develop systems that improve customer acquisition, marketing performance, sales processes, reporting, and business intelligence. Continuously identify opportunities to optimize workflows, reduce operational costs, and improve overall business performance. Qualifications Required Strong knowledge of AI tools and practical AI implementation. Experience with automation platforms such as Zapier, Make.com, GoHighLevel, or similar. Experience working with APIs, webhooks, and third-party integrations. Hands-on experience with GoHighLevel (GHL). Experience managing business social media accounts and digital marketing campaigns. Understanding of major social media platforms, analytics, scheduling tools, and content strategies. Experience supporting e-commerce businesses or online marketplaces. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and systems-thinking abilities. Excellent organizational and time management skills. Ability to work independently while meeting deadlines. Strong written and verbal communication skills. Preferred Experience designing end-to-end business automation systems. Background in AI implementation or systems architecture. Experience integrating multiple SaaS platforms. Familiarity with Shopify, WooCommerce, Amazon, Walmart Marketplace, Etsy, or similar platforms. Experience with prompt engineering and AI-assisted content creation. Experience creating dashboards, reporting systems, and business analytics. Understanding of SEO, paid advertising, and digital marketing automation is a plus. Hiring Process Our hiring process focuses on demonstrated ability rather than interviews alone. The process includes: Application review Initial interview (for shortlisted candidates) Paid production-based trial assignment involving real business tasks representative of the role Evaluation based on: Quality of work Problem-solving ability Accuracy and attention to detail Timeliness Communication Initiative and ability to work independently Final hiring decision and onboarding Successful completion of the paid trial assignment is required before full onboarding. This process allows both you and our team to determine whether the position is the right long-term fit. What You Can Expect Starting rate of $5.00/hour Performance and revenue review after 90 days Opportunity for an increase to $6.00/hour based on performance and contribution Long-term remote position Growth opportunities as our businesses expand Opportunity to contribute to multiple companies within our digital mall ecosystem Exposure to cutting-edge AI technologies, automation systems, CRM development, digital marketing, and e-commerce operations Collaborative environment focused on innovation, efficiency, continuous improvement, and measurable business results About the Company We are building a multi-business digital ecosystem powered by AI, automation, scalable operational systems, digital marketing, and intelligent business processes. Our mission is to create an integrated infrastructure that connects multiple companies through automation, technology, and revenue-driven execution. We value professionals who are proactive, systems-oriented, resourceful, execution-focused, and committed to building long-term solutions that create measurable business impact. Application Link: forms.gle/n3MHgVGeinh3B2Zg9

Full job description

About the Role: We are looking for a highly skilled and proactive AI Specialist from the Philippines to join our team. We are currently developing custom AI solutions designed to support our district managers and leadership teams with daily operational audits, policy compliance, and employee performance tracking. As we transition our initial AI prototypes into company-wide rollouts, we need an expert to help us scale these systems, automate day-to-day workflows, and seamlessly integrate AI tools across our various departments (including operations and marketing) and existing tech stack. Work Schedule: This is a remote position that requires working during standard business hours in Eastern Standard Time (EST). Key Responsibilities: AI Development & Scaling: Manage and refine existing conversational AI models, optimizing prompts to extract actionable insights from standard daily operational reports. Data Architecture & Integration: Architect and manage scalable cloud integrations (e.g., mapping custom models to a master corporate OneDrive) to bypass native AI file upload constraints and establish a unified knowledge base. Data Integrity & Maintenance: Continuously clean, structure, and update backend training data to eliminate AI hallucinations, ensuring all technical and operational guidance is 100?curate. Workflow & Document Automation: Develop automated systems that seamlessly format conversational AI outputs into compliant, exportable, print-ready documents (such as PDFs or Word files) directly from the interface. Privacy & Security Controls: Implement robust security protocols and access controls to isolate chat histories on shared workplace devices, safeguarding sensitive corporate data and personal information. Multi-Platform Integration & Programming: Evaluate and program integrations with alternative advanced AI engines (such as Anthropic Claude, Google Gemini, or others) and automation frameworks (like n8n) to continuously expand and upgrade our system's capabilities. Multimedia Expansion: Assist in building out dynamic resources, including mapping text-based troubleshooting prompts to external instructional video playlists. Required Skills & Qualifications: - Proven experience building, configuring, and scaling Custom GPTs and AI chatbots. - Strong understanding of the Microsoft 365 ecosystem (specifically OneDrive integration with AI platforms). - Experience exploring or building with alternative AI models like Anthropic Claude or Google Gemini. - Experience with prompt engineering methodologies (Context, Person, Results framework is a plus!). - Familiarity with workflow automation tools (e.g., n8n, Zapier, Make). - Ability to troubleshoot latency issues and optimize AI response times for fast-paced work environments. - Excellent problem-solving skills, particularly regarding data privacy, user access controls, and dynamic document generation. - Must be available to work dedicated hours in Eastern Standard Time (EST). To Apply: Please include examples of past AI bots or automation workflows you have built, particularly any projects involving Microsoft 365/OneDrive integrations, multi-platform AI use (e.g., Claude, Gemini), or dynamic document generation.

Full job description

About CrowdReply CrowdReply is an AI brand visibility platform that helps brands see and improve how AI models like ChatGPT, Google AI Mode, Perplexity and Gemini talk about them. We're operating in one of the most exciting, fast-moving spaces in marketing right now: AEO is still being defined, the playbook is being written in real time, and backlinks + brand mentions are becoming a core signal that feeds AI answers. Because the space is evolving so quickly, growth opportunities here are significant. You'll be joining early, taking real ownership, and helping build the link-building and brand-mention engine from the ground up with plenty of room to grow into a broader off-page/authority-building leadership role as we scale. About the Role We're looking for a Backlink Outreach Specialist to take full ownership of CrowdReply's link acquisition engine. Your single mission: consistently land high-quality backlinks and brand mentions that grow our domain authority and organic visibility. This is a hands-on, results-driven role. You'll own the entire outreach process from sourcing and qualifying prospects, to finding contacts, running email campaigns, and closing placements. You won't be writing on-site articles, doing technical audits, or handling on-page SEO. Your job is purely to build a strong, diverse, and clean backlink profile. We're a tech company with an in-house dev team, so if a part of your workflow can be automated, we'll build it for you. We'll also provide all the tools and access you need. What You'll Own - Campaign strategy & setup: Plan and run end-to-end link building campaigns with clear targets and timelines. - Prospecting & site sourcing: Identify high-quality, relevant, and authoritative websites using tools like Ahrefs and SEMrush (analyzing DR/DA, organic traffic, topical relevance, spam signals). - Contact discovery: Find and verify decision-maker contact details using Snov.io, Hunter, or similar tools. - Email outreach: Build, launch, and optimize outreach sequences on platforms like Lemlist, Instantly, or Smartlead — managing deliverability, follow-ups, and reply handling. - Link acquisition tactics: Execute across listicle outreach, guest post publishing, and niche edits. - Anchor text & diversification: Own anchor text strategy and maintain a natural, diversified, penalty-safe link profile. - Quality control: Identify and avoid toxic/spammy backlinks; prioritize high-authority, relevant placements. - Reporting: Track links landed, placement quality, reply rates, and campaign ROI. Must-Have Requirements - Proven experience running backlink outreach campaigns end-to-end with a track record of landed links. - Hands-on expertise with Ahrefs and/or SEMrush for prospecting and backlink analysis. - Experience with contact-finding tools (Snov.io, Hunter, or equivalents). - Experience with cold email / outreach platforms (Lemlist, Instantly, Smartlead, or similar), including deliverability best practices. - Strong grasp of anchor text strategy and link diversification. - Ability to distinguish toxic vs. high-quality backlinks. - Fluent with AI tools especially Claude — to speed up prospecting, personalization, filtering, and overall productivity. This is core to the role, not optional. - Excellent written English and self-management; able to take ownership with minimal supervision. Nice-to-Have - Familiarity with Parasite SEO — bonus if you've actually executed it. - Experience automating outreach workflows (n8n, Zapier, Make, scripts, etc.). - Existing relationships with publishers/site owners in relevant niches. What You WON'T Be Doing - Writing articles for our website - On-page / technical SEO or technical audits - Any on-site SEO work Your sole focus is acquiring high-quality backlinks and brand mentions for CrowdReply. What We Provide - All tools and access you need (Ahrefs, SEMrush, outreach platforms, contact finders, etc.) - Dev team support to automate parts of your workflow and maximize your output Performance based bonus details will be discussed once you're shortlisted When sending your application, answer this: What is 9+13?

Full job description

Creative Strategist — KYOR (Performance Creative) 1) About Us Symple Wellness created KYOR, a science-backed wellness brand focused on clean formulations, clinically studied ingredients, and real results. Our hero product line is designed to simplify skincare and boost whole-body wellness — empowering individuals to glow confidently from the inside out. Website: kyor.co/ We are rapidly expanding, especially in the US market, and we're looking for a sharp, analytical, performance-driven **Creative Strategist** who lives and breathes paid social. This is the person who decides *what* we say, *who* we say it to, and *why* it'll convert — then writes the briefs that turn that thinking into ads our designers and editors can build. You won't be pushing pixels in Photoshop all day. Your output is the strategy and the brief: the angle, the persona, the hook, the concept, the shotlist. The visuals follow your thinking. This is one of the highest-leverage roles on the team — get the brief right and everything downstream scales; get it wrong and we burn spend on beautiful ads that don't sell. 2) What You'll Do - Write 20+ creative briefs per week that translate strategy into clear, buildable direction for designers and video editors across Meta (Feed, Reels, Stories), TikTok, and other platforms - Develop distinct messaging angles mapped to specific personas, pain points, and awareness stages — not "glow from within" mush, but sharp, differentiated concepts that filter for the right buyer - Research relentlessly: tear down competitor ads, build and maintain swipe files, study what's winning in beauty/wellness feeds, and reverse-engineer *why* it works - Define hooks, concepts, and formats for each brief — including the first 3 seconds, the scroll-stopper, and the CTA - Write UGC scripts, listicle scripts, and shotlists when a brief needs them, and spec AI image/video prompts clearly enough that a designer can execute without guessing - Partner with designers, copywriters, and our influencer/KOL team to ideate and pressure-test concepts before they go into production - Read the data and let it drive the next brief: hook rate, CTR, CPA, ROAS, thumbstop. Kill what's losing, double down on what's winning, and brief accordingly - Build and extend the frameworks, templates, and naming conventions that let the team move fast and stay organised 3) What You Bring **Must-haves** - 2+ year working on paid social creative — as a strategist, brief-writer, media buyer who briefs creative, UGC strategist, agency creative, or a strong self-directed track record (your own ads, teardowns, or a brand you've run creative for all count) - Strategic thinking with an actual framework — you reach for persona ? pain ? angle ? hook ? format, not vibes. When you watch an ad you can say *precisely* why it works or doesn't - Strong, clear writing. A brief is only useful if a designer can build from it without a meeting. You write tight, specific, and unambiguous - Working understanding of how paid social performs: what hook rate, CTR, CPA, and ROAS mean, and how creative moves them - The ability to generate volume, not just curate. Performance creative is a numbers game — you can produce 15 hooks fast, then know which 3 are worth shooting - Comfort taking direct, critical feedback and iterating without ego. The job is briefing creative against data feedback every single week, often when the data says your favourite concept flopped. You revise, you don't sulk - Hungry to learn, genuinely curious about why people buy, and energised by the idea that your work is measured by performance, not praise 4) AI Proficiency (required) We expect everyone on this team to use AI to think faster, research deeper, and produce more. You should already be comfortable with — or eager to learn — the tools we use day to day: - **Ideation, research & brief-writing:** ChatGPT, Claude, or similar — for brainstorming angles, structuring briefs, analysing reviews and competitor ads, and pressure-testing concepts - **Prompt engineering for production:** you can write image/video prompts precise enough for a designer to run through Nano Banana (Google Gemini's image model), ChatGPT Images, Seedance 2.0, or Veo 3 and get usable output - **Workflow:** you use AI to compress research and first drafts so you spend your time on the thinking that matters, not the busywork In the interview process, you'll be asked to show how AI actually shows up in your workflow — not that you've tried these tools, but how you've integrated them into how you strategise and write. If you haven't used our exact stack, that's fine — you should have hands-on experience with comparable tools and be ready to pick ours up quickly. **Nice-to-haves** - Personal interest in beauty, wellness, or skincare (you're a KYOR target customer or close to one) - Familiarity with supplement/beauty compliance — claim substantiation, on-label mechanism language, the difference between a sellable claim and a liability - Experience with Meta Ads Manager, creative testing, or structured ad scorecards - Hands-on UGC and influencer/KOL experience — scripting, shotlists, working with creators - An eye for art direction — you can't make the ad, but you know what good looks like and can point a designer toward it ## 5) How to Apply Send the following to teamsymple@gmail.com with the subject line: "I Am Your Creative Strategist — [Your Name]" - Your CV - A link to your work — a portfolio, swipe deck, ad teardown thread, briefs you've written, ads you've strategised, or a brand you've run creative for (Behance, Notion, Google Drive, PDF, or social all fine) - A 3–5 sentence note on which AI tools you currently use in your strategy/research workflow and how We respond to every application within 5 working days. Shortlisted candidates will go through a short three-round process: a 15-minute personality profiling assessment, followed by 2 strategy tests to understand how you think.

Full job description

If your instinct when something breaks is to fix the pipeline before fixing the symptom — this role was built for you. To apply please fill out the application form here: u247.us/bepartofourteam. About the company We're a family-owned business with all-local Philippines leadership and team. We've been 100% work-from-home since day one — not a pandemic pivot. We've grown by valuing our people, building long-term relationships, and doing great work for great clients. The Role You're not here to deploy code and move on. You're here to make sure the systems that deliver that code are reliable, secure, automated, and continuously improving. We're looking for a DevOps Engineer who thinks in pipelines, not tasks. You'll own the infrastructure that keeps our development and client environments running — CI/CD pipelines, Linux systems, cloud platforms, monitoring, and security. When something breaks, you find the root cause. When something works, you ask how to make it more reliable, more automated, and less dependent on human intervention next time. The Mindset We're Looking For The best DevOps engineers we've worked with share a few traits: They treat manual processes as technical debt. If something requires a human to do it repeatedly, their instinct is to eliminate that dependency — not just document it They take ownership. From the moment an infrastructure problem is theirs, it's theirs until it's resolved, documented, and hardened against happening again They think in systems. They don't just fix what's broken — they ask what else could break, and why, and build accordingly They communicate clearly. Status, blockers, and deployment risks are shared openly — before being asked for They keep learning. The DevOps landscape moves fast. Twelve months from now they know more than they do today — because they made sure of it Who Thrives Here People who are technically sharp, operationally minded, and genuinely excited by the challenge of building infrastructure that just works. You don't wait to be handed a list of things to automate — you find them. You're comfortable with ambiguity, resourceful under pressure, and you care more about system reliability than staying in a defined lane. We're a growing MSP, which means not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. For the right engineer, that's not a warning — it's the opportunity to build things the right way from the ground up. What You'll Do You'll walk into an environment where development pipelines, Linux infrastructure, cloud systems, and client integrations all need an engineer who can own them end-to-end. Your job isn't just to keep things running — it's to make them run better, more reliably, and with less human intervention every month. That looks like: Designing, building, and maintaining CI/CD pipelines that make deployments fast, reliable, and repeatable — so shipping code is a non-event, not a risk Managing and administering Linux servers (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) across development and client environments — knowing these systems well enough to troubleshoot them at any layer Administering web servers (Nginx, Apache) and databases — keeping them performant, secure, and maintainable Implementing client-requested integrations — understanding what's being asked, translating it into a reliable technical solution, and delivering it cleanly Conducting root cause analysis on production errors — not just fixing what's visible, but understanding what allowed it to happen and building the safeguard that prevents it Building scripts and automation (Bash required; Python and PHP a plus) that eliminate repetitive manual work from the team's daily operations Monitoring infrastructure, resource utilization, and security events — building alerting systems that catch problems before they become incidents Designing and enforcing DevSecOps practices — making security a built-in part of the pipeline, not an afterthought Working with cloud platforms and virtualization — managing hypervisors and cloud service providers with the confidence of someone who's done it in production What We're Looking For Proven experience in Linux server administration (Ubuntu, CentOS, Debian) — you know these systems well enough to manage, troubleshoot, and optimize them without a guide Hands-on CI/CD experience — you've built and maintained real pipelines, not just used them; you know what makes a deployment safe and what makes it a liability Strong version control administration — Git, remote repositories, branching strategies, code review workflows; you manage these with intention, not just habit Web server administration experience — Nginx and Apache, configuration, performance tuning, and troubleshooting Database administration experience — you can manage database servers, optimize queries, and keep data environments healthy Bash scripting proficiency (required) — you write automation that works, that others can read, and that makes the team faster; Python and PHP experience is a plus Solid cloud computing and virtualization experience — working with hypervisors and cloud service providers (AWS, GCP, Azure, or equivalent) in real production environments Infrastructure and event monitoring experience — you build alerting systems that surface the right information at the right time, not noise Understanding of DevSecOps — you treat security as part of the build, not something bolted on at the end Strong diagnostic skills — you find the root cause of a production issue, not just the fastest path to closing the ticket Self-directed once you understand the environment — but you ask smart questions early to get there Honest communicator — you surface risks and blockers clearly and early, even when the news isn't good Long-term mindset — you want to build something that lasts, not move on when things get complex How Onboarding Works (And How That Changes) We're honest about how we work with new team members. In your first weeks and months, you'll have real hands-on support from the team. We'll work closely with you to understand our infrastructure, our standards, our tools, and how we operate. We expect questions — and we'll give you the time to learn. As you show what you can do — and you will — that involvement steps back quickly. Trust grows fast here. People who demonstrate ownership, judgment, and quality work earn autonomy quickly. People who consistently deliver end up with significant latitude to shape the infrastructure, the pipelines, and how we build. We don't want to over-direct people who don't need it. But we also don't believe in throwing someone in without context and calling it autonomy. We earn each other's trust through real work — then we get out of the way. What Success Looks Like At three months: You have a clear picture of the infrastructure landscape — what's healthy, what's fragile, and what needs to be rebuilt. At least one significant manual process has been automated. Your documentation is already what the team references. At six months: Deployments are more reliable and less stressful than when you joined. Monitoring is catching problems before they become incidents. The team is spending less time on reactive firefighting and more time building. At twelve months: The infrastructure is measurably more stable, secure, and automated than before you joined. You've built systems the team depends on — and you're already identifying the next layer of improvement. The company is investing in your next step. How We Work We trust our people to do great work and we give them the space to do it. There's real work to be done — clients and internal teams depend on the infrastructure you manage — but we understand that doing meaningful work means making decisions, and sometimes those decisions won't be right on the first try. That's how we find the right path. We don't punish thoughtful mistakes. We learn from them and move forward. We're a growing MSP, so not every pipeline is optimized and not every process is documented yet. That's part of why we need someone like you — to help us build the infrastructure, automations, and systems that make us better as we grow. This Role Is Not for You If: You need a defined list of tasks to know what to work on You deploy the fix without asking what caused the failure You treat documentation and runbooks as someone else's job You're comfortable with fragile systems as long as they're currently running Your outside commitments regularly affect your availability and focus You're looking for a low-pressure remote role with predictable, well-defined work There's no judgment in that list — it's just not the environment. We'd rather be honest now than waste each other's time. What We Offer Competitive salary based on experience 100% remote — no corporate commute, ever Weekly pay Tech and internet allowance Paid vacation leave HMO Access to the latest AI tools (Claude, Code, and more) to work smarter and automate more Career growth

Full job description

Executive Assistant / Chief of Staff to Real Estate Entrepreneur This is a fairly long job post requiring careful reading, and there are many questions to answer as part of this process. It may take some time but it could very well be worth it because we are urgently hiring an awesome person to join our excellent team immediately! We offer excellent compensation for the right people. We are ready to hire the right individual immediately. We are a growing real estate investment, property management, and title company organization based in Miami, Florida. Our companies own and manage over 100 residential rental units, including multifamily, single-family rentals, Section 8 housing, and short-term rental properties. We also operate a successful title company and are actively acquiring additional properties. We are looking for an exceptional Executive Assistant / Chief of Staff who can become a long-term strategic partner to the founder. This is not an entry-level assistant position. You will help organize, manage, and improve operations across multiple businesses while helping the founder stay focused on the highest-value activities. We are looking for someone who enjoys solving problems, taking ownership, creating systems, and making things happen without constant supervision. The Right Person is: Extremely organized Highly intelligent Detail-oriented Resourceful Trustworthy Proactive Comfortable figuring things out independently Excellent at written and verbal communication Calm under pressure Able to manage many projects simultaneously You don't wait for instructions. You identify problems and bring solutions. You follow through relentlessly until tasks are completed. You double-check your work before submitting it. You think like an owner. Responsibilities: Executive Support Calendar management Meeting coordination Travel planning Event coordination Inbox management Follow-up management Vendor coordination Personal scheduling Real Estate Operations Track acquisitions and due diligence Coordinate inspections Software implementation Track contract deadlines Coordinate lenders, attorneys, brokers, title companies, and other real estate vendors Maintain deal pipeline reports Assist with underwriting and research Prepare meeting materials Property Management Support Monitor property management KPIs Track vacancies Track leasing performance Coordinate maintenance vendors Follow up on tenant issues Improve operating procedures Help build and document systems Project Management Assistance with health and wellness matters Manage special projects from start to finish Track deliverables and deadlines Create accountability systems Build SOPs and workflows Market research Vendor research Property research Competitive analysis AI tools and automation research Problem-solving assignments Draft emails Prepare reports Communicate with vendors Coordinate internal teams Follow up until completion Required Skills: Exceptional written English Strong business communication skills Excellent organizational ability Advanced Google Workspace skills Strong Microsoft Office skills Project management experience Ability to learn new software quickly Strong internet research skills Bonus Qualifications: Experience with: Real estate investing Property management Section 8 housing Multifamily operations Airbnb / short-term rentals Title companies Monday.com ClickUp Asana Airtable Notion ChatGPT and AI tools EOS operating system What Success Looks Like Within 90 days: Founder inbox under control Calendars fully organized Critical tasks never missed Project tracking system implemented Weekly executive dashboard created Follow-up system running consistently SOP library started Team accountability improved Within 12 months: Become one of the founder's trusted inner circle individuals Manage major projects independently Help scale operations Reduce founder workload significantly Improve execution across all companies Compensation: Based on experience and capability. We are willing to pay significantly more for exceptional talent who can truly own outcomes and become a long-term partner. Application Questions: To be considered for this position, please answer all of the following questions in corresponding numbered answers and in detail. Generic or AI-generated answers will not be considered. Important: To confirm that you carefully read this entire job posting, begin your application with the words "Blue Mango." 1. Employment History How many employers have you worked for during the last 10 years? For each employer, please provide: Company name Job title Employment dates Reason for leaving What was the longest position you have ever held? 2. Executive Support Experience Describe the most demanding executive, entrepreneur, or business owner you have ever supported. Please include: Industry Company size Number of employees Number of projects managed simultaneously What made the role challenging 3. Problem-Solving Tell us about a major problem you identified before your manager, executive, or business owner became aware of it. What was the problem? What actions did you take? What was the outcome? 4. Last 3 Positions Why did you leave your last three positions? If you were terminated why? And if you resigned what could those employers have done to keep you longer? 5.. High-Pressure Work Environment Describe the busiest period of your career. Approximately how many projects, clients, properties, team members, or deadlines were you responsible for simultaneously? How did you stay organized? 6. Technology & Software Experience List every major software platform you have used professionally during the last three years. For each platform, rate your proficiency from 1–10. 7. Artificial Intelligence Experience How do you currently use AI tools in your work? Please provide at least three specific examples. 8.. Real Estate Experience Describe any experience you have with: Real estate investing Property management Multifamily housing Section 8 housing Construction or renovations Real estate acquisitions Lending Title companies Airbnb and short term rental properties Real estate law Please provide specific examples. 9.. Difficult People Describe the most difficult person you have ever had to work with. How did you handle the situation? What was the outcome? 10. Career Goals Where do you see yourself in three years? Where do you see yourself in five years? What would make you stay with one company for 10+ years? 11. Ownership Mentality Tell us about a situation where you inherited something that was disorganized, broken, inefficient, or failing. What specifically did you do to improve it? What measurable results did you achieve? 12. Compensation What annual compensation are you seeking? What compensation range would make you excited to stay with a company long term? 13. Availability How many hours per week are you currently available? What is your time zone? Are you willing to work U.S. Eastern Time business hours? 14. Inbox Management Test How many unread emails are currently in your inbox? What system do you personally use to ensure important items never fall through the cracks? 15. Data Have you ever had access to company bank accounts, financial information, confidential legal matters, passwords, payroll data, or sensitive business information? If so, describe your responsibilities. 16. Processes/Systems Describe a time you built a process or system that significantly improved operations. And do you have experience creating SOP's or implementing any operating procedures within an organization? 17. Term Why are you interested in a long-term position rather than freelancing? 18. EA Experience What direct experience have you had as an Executive Assistant?

Full job description

ABOUT THE ROLE We're building out our organic growth engine — affiliate partnerships, citation acquisition, and AI-assisted content — and we need someone to drive this work at scale. This role lives in research, spreadsheets, outreach databases, and analytics tools. You'll be prospecting YouTube creators and travel bloggers, building relationships through Commission Junction, finding citation and backlink opportunities, and eventually helping shape our AI content workflow. This is not a 'post on social media' marketing role. It's data-heavy, research-intensive, and requires someone who can sit with a project for days, build systems around it, and execute thousands of outreach touchpoints methodically. WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING Affiliate Network Growth (YouTube + Travel Blogs) • Research YouTube channels in the RV rentals, RV ownership, and travel/tourism space • Research and outreach to campgrounds and RV parks to expose and promote the brand • Identify contact information (typically in About sections) and build a structured prospect database • Reach out using approved scripts to invite creators to join our affiliate program via Commission Junction (CJ) • Apply the same research and outreach methodology to travel blogs and niche websites • Track outreach-to-conversion rates in CJ and optimize the approach over time Citation & Backlink Acquisition • Identify websites that mention competitors or geo-relevant RV content but don't yet reference our brands. • Evaluate domain authority and relevance using SEMrush and other SEO research tools • Reach out to site owners with content snippets or requests for new pages — building us up as an authority source for LLMs and search engines • Maintain a database of acquired citations and track progress over time AI Content Support • Assist in developing prompt templates for AI-generated content (blog posts, web pages, product descriptions) • Generate drafts using AI tools based on approved brand voice guidelines and structured prompts • Coordinate fact-checking with internal subject matter experts before content is published TOOLS YOU'LL WORK WITH • SEMrush (or equivalent) for keyword research, competitive gap analysis, and backlink research • Commission Junction for affiliate program management • ChatGPT / Claude or similar AI tools for research assistance and content drafting • Google Sheets / Airtable for prospect databases and outreach tracking • Email outreach tools for scaled communication YOU'RE A GREAT FIT IF YOU... • Are analytical, patient, and comfortable with repetitive, data-heavy work • Have 1–3 years of experience in SEO, digital marketing, content outreach, or affiliate marketing • Can work independently and build processes — not just follow them • Understand what 'domain authority' means and how organic search works • Communicate clearly in English and can represent us professionally in outreach • Are comfortable using AI tools as a productivity layer, not a crutch POTENTIAL TO GROW INTO • Full ownership of our content production pipeline • Managing external freelancers for content and SEO work • Strategic SEO planning alongside our existing agency

Full job description

Caliber Workforce | Operations Architect (Home Services) APPLY HERE: www.caliberworkforce.com/aicareers Caliber Workforce is a premium, AI-fluent staffing platform for founder-operators at growth-stage businesses. We don't place generic virtual assistants. We place vetted operators into defined roles with the Caliber Operating System: a role-specific Toolkit, a 90-day Playbook, continuous AI training, and market intelligence. We're placing an Operations Architect into a U.S.-based home services business. This is not a general admin role. You'll design the operational backbone: SOPs, automations, project portfolios, and the cross-functional handoffs that keep the business running. You'll give the founder cleaner processes, clearer handoffs, and better internal coordination, using AI to build systems that scale past manual effort. WHAT YOU'LL OWN: Design and document the operational backbone using off-the-shelf tools Build SOPs that standardize how the business runs, then keep them current Build automations that remove manual, repetitive work across the operation Own the project portfolio: priorities, deadlines, and status visible at a glance Map and tighten cross-functional handoffs between office, field, and vendors Use AI to design workflows, draft documentation, and surface bottlenecks faster Monitor process performance and flag breakdowns before they cost jobs or revenue Roll out new systems with the team and make sure they actually get adopted TOOLS YOU SHOULD KNOW (some or all): Notion, ClickUp, or Asana for SOPs and project portfolios ServiceTitan, Jobber, or Housecall Pro (home services systems) Zapier or Make for workflow automation Google Workspace or Microsoft 365 Claude and ChatGPT (AI fluency is tested, not assumed) Slack, Zoom, or phone/SMS coordination WHO WE'RE LOOKING FOR: 2+ years in operations, process design, systems, or business operations AI-fluent: you already use Claude or ChatGPT to design and document systems, and you can build a multi-step automation Strong written and verbal English communication, with clear documentation instincts Home services, trades, or field service experience strongly preferred You think in systems and spot the process behind the chaos You manage multiple workstreams simultaneously without losing track You're proactive about flagging process breakdowns and fixing them before they escalate Comfortable working U.S. business hours aligned with your client's time zone Stable internet connection and a professional remote work setup HOW CALIBER MATCHES YOU: Every operator goes through an 81-question behavioral assessment across 6 dimensions, a role-specific skills assessment, and an AI-transcribed video interview reviewed by Claude We place at an 85% minimum match score. You're not just available. You're matched to a role and a client where you'll perform and grow. WHAT CALIBER PROVIDES: Full-time, long-term placement at 40 hours per week. Not project-based or seasonal. 5-day onboarding with the Caliber Operating System. You start the Monday after your client signs. A role-specific Toolkit: prompt libraries, SOP templates, and workflow templates tuned to home services operations A 90-day Playbook with structured check-ins at Day 1, Week 1, Day 30, and Day 90, then quarterly Quarterly AI training so your skills stay current as the tools change 10 days PTO annually after the 90-day mark, plus 5 sick days available immediately A company that invests in your success, not just your output WHY CALIBER IS DIFFERENT: Most offshore staffing companies hand you a title and disappear. Caliber doesn't. You're matched on evidence, not availability. 81 questions. 6 dimensions. 85% minimum match. You get a role-specific operating system, continuous AI training, and a 90-day Playbook that tells both you and your client exactly where the placement landed. We treat you as an operator, not a commodity. HOW TO APPLY: APPLY HERE: www.caliberworkforce.com/aicareers Complete your application through our platform. The process includes a behavioral assessment that matches you to the right role and the right client. No generic applications. No guessing. If you're ready to be placed, not just hired, apply now. www.caliberworkforce.com/aicareers

Full job description

## Job Title: **SEO Specialist with Google Ads & Web Design Experience** ## Job Description Digital NinjaZ is a U.S.-based IT and digital marketing company located in Florida. We help small businesses with managed IT, cybersecurity, websites, SEO, Google Ads, CCTV/security cameras, networking, and general technology support. We are looking for a reliable **SEO Specialist** who can help with SEO, backlink research, Google Ads support, website updates, and basic web design tasks. This position is remote, but some work may require logging into a secure computer located in our Florida office using remote desktop software. You must have **strong English reading and writing skills**. You need to understand written instructions, read client website content, write clear SEO content, and communicate with our U.S. team without constant clarification. Good spoken English is preferred for training calls and occasional meetings. We are also looking for someone who is comfortable using **AI tools like ChatGPT** to improve speed, research, content writing, SEO planning, and reporting. ## Responsibilities * Perform on-page SEO updates * Research keywords for local service businesses * Help write and improve website content * Create or update service pages * Optimize page titles, meta descriptions, headings, and image alt text * Assist with backlink research and outreach * Build and clean up local citations/directories * Help manage Google Business Profile optimization * Assist with Google Ads campaigns * Review Google Search Console and Google Analytics data * Help update WordPress, Wix, or other small business websites * Make basic web design edits * Prepare simple SEO reports * Use AI tools to speed up research, writing, and optimization * Track completed work and send daily updates ## Required Skills * SEO experience * Strong English reading and writing skills * Basic Google Ads knowledge * WordPress or Wix experience * Basic web design skills * Ability to follow detailed written instructions * Comfortable using AI tools like ChatGPT * Organized and detail-oriented * Reliable internet connection * Comfortable using remote desktop software * Available for some overlap with Florida Eastern Time ## Preferred Skills * Local SEO experience * Google Business Profile optimization * Backlink outreach * Citation building * Canva or basic graphic design * HTML/CSS basics * Experience with small business websites * Experience with tools like Google Analytics, Google Search Console, Ahrefs, SEMrush, Ubersuggest, Screaming Frog, or similar tools ## Work Schedule We prefer someone who can work **6–8 hours per day**, Monday to Friday. Some overlap with **Florida Eastern Time, USA** is required for communication, training, and task review. Occasional voice or video calls may be required during Florida business hours. ## Pay Pay depends on experience. Please include your requested hourly rate or monthly salary when applying. We prefer to start with a **paid trial period** before offering a long-term position. ## Important Requirements Please do not apply if you are not comfortable with: * Strong English reading and writing * Using remote desktop software * Using AI tools as part of your workflow * Tracking your completed work * Sending daily work summaries * Joining occasional voice or video calls * Starting with a paid trial period * Following step-by-step instructions ## How to Apply Please answer the following questions in English: 1. Tell us about your SEO experience. 2. Send examples of websites you have worked on. 3. What SEO tools have you used? 4. What Google Ads experience do you have? 5. What website platforms have you worked with? WordPress, Wix, Shopify, etc. 6. What AI tools do you use? 7. Are you comfortable using remote desktop software? 8. Are you comfortable joining occasional voice or video calls in English? 9. What hours can you work in Florida Eastern Time? 10. What is your requested hourly rate or monthly salary? 11. What are the first 5 SEO tasks you would perform for a local service business website? Use this subject line when applying: **SEO Specialist - Digital NinjaZ** Applications with short, copy-pasted, or poorly written answers will be ignored.

Senior WordPress Developer / Technical SEO Specialist – Full-Time Remote

Full Time 40 hrs/week Posted/updated: Jun 25, 2026 AUD $10/h $1,732/mo est. Senior ask, but trainable profile language

We are looking for a highly capable **WordPress developer with strong technical SEO experience** to help repair, optimise and grow an established Australian marine/classifieds website. This is **not** a basic web design role. We need someone who can improve...

Why it belongs here
  • AI/build signals: ai tools, llm
  • Build surface: website, websites, landing page, repo, html, css, javascript, api
  • Code/build substrate: repo, html, css, javascript, api, cloudflare
  • Trainable language: va, learn

Arca angle
  • Full-time role: good fit for a dedicated Arca graduate placement.
Full job description

We are looking for a highly capable **WordPress developer with strong technical SEO experience** to help repair, optimise and grow an established Australian marine/classifieds website. This is **not** a basic web design role. We need someone who can improve website performance, indexing, crawlability, site architecture, structured data, rankings and lead generation. The first project is a **4–6 week full-time SEO recovery and website optimisation project**. For the right person, this may become a long-term role helping build and optimise a group of related marketplace/classifieds websites. ## Experience Required Minimum requirement: * 5+ years professional WordPress development experience * Strong technical SEO experience * Experience with Google Search Console * Experience improving Core Web Vitals and page speed * Experience troubleshooting WordPress themes, plugins and site architecture Preferred: * 7+ years WordPress / PHP / technical SEO experience * Experience with listing, directory, marketplace, real estate, automotive, marine or classifieds websites * Experience recovering websites that have lost rankings or indexing visibility ## Main Responsibilities You will be responsible for: * Technical SEO audit * WordPress theme/plugin review * Google Search Console review * Google Analytics review * Fixing indexing and crawlability issues * Improving sitemap, robots.txt, redirects and canonical tags * Improving Core Web Vitals and page speed * Mobile optimisation * Image optimisation * Internal linking improvements * URL structure improvements * Listing/detail page SEO improvements * Category and location landing page strategy * Schema / structured data implementation * Conversion improvements for enquiry forms * Basic CRM/form integration where needed * Daily progress updates * Weekly performance reports ## Required Skills You must have strong experience with: * WordPress development * PHP * HTML * CSS * JavaScript * Technical SEO * Google Search Console * Google Analytics * Core Web Vitals * Schema markup / structured data * Custom post types * Custom taxonomies * Mobile optimisation * Page-speed optimisation * Plugin/theme troubleshooting * Website backups and security ## Preferred Experience Experience with any of the following is highly valued: * Classified websites * Directory websites * Marketplace websites * Listing/inventory websites * Real estate, automotive or marine websites * HubSpot * Zapier * Google Tag Manager * Rank Math / Yoast * Elementor * Cloudflare * Local SEO * Programmatic SEO * AI search / LLM search optimisation ## AI Search / LLM Optimisation We also want the website structured for modern AI search and answer engines. You should understand, or be willing to learn deeply about: * Answer engine optimisation * Entity-based SEO * Structured data for AI systems * Clear expert content architecture * FAQ and Q&A content structures * Topical authority * Comparison pages * Citation-worthy content * Making a business easy for AI tools to understand and recommend ## Communication Requirement Proactive and clear communication is a must. We need someone who: * provides updates without being chased * explains technical issues clearly * flags problems early * asks clear questions when needed * gives realistic timelines * does not hide blockers * documents what has been changed * can explain the commercial reason behind technical work This role requires someone who can work independently and communicate like a professional. Silent workers, vague updates and unclear reporting will not be suitable. ## Daily Reporting Daily reporting is required. At the end of each workday, send a short update covering: * what you worked on * what you completed * issues found * fixes made * what you will work on next * anything needed from us Daily reports must be specific. For example, do not write “worked on SEO”. Explain exactly what was checked, fixed or improved. ## Weekly Reporting Weekly reporting is also required, covering: * ranking movement * Search Console changes * indexed pages * speed improvements * Core Web Vitals changes * completed work * remaining issues * next priorities * blockers or risks ## Pay and Hours Rate: **AUD $10 per hour** Hours: **Full-time preferred** Initial project: **4–6 weeks** Long-term role possible for the right person ## Ideal Candidate We are looking for someone who is: * a serious developer, not just a designer * strong in technical SEO * detail-oriented * reliable * proactive * analytical * commercially minded * able to work independently * clear in communication * focused on measurable results * comfortable giving daily updates * willing to take ownership of outcomes ## How To Apply Please answer the following: 1. Briefly describe your WordPress development experience. 2. How many years of professional WordPress experience do you have? 3. Describe your technical SEO experience. 4. Share examples of websites where you improved SEO, speed, indexing or rankings. 5. What is your experience with Google Search Console? 6. What is your experience with Core Web Vitals? 7. What is your experience with schema / structured data? 8. Have you worked on classified, directory, marketplace or listing websites? 9. What do you know about AI search / LLM search optimisation? 10. What are the first 10 things you would check on a website that has lost Google rankings? 11. How do you normally communicate progress on a technical project? 12. What are your usual working hours and internet speed? ## Attention-to-Detail Test At the very top of your application, write this exact phrase: **TECHNICAL SEO RECOVERY - MARINE** Also include one sentence explaining the difference between technical SEO and on-page SEO. Applications that do not include the exact phrase and explanation will not be considered.

Full job description

About Us We're an arbitrage sports-betting company building the systems that find edges, place bets, and manage operations automatically. We use AI throughout the business and are investing heavily in models and automation. You'd be helping build the engine. The Role You'll develop the software and models behind our betting. That means building predictive sports-betting models, automating bet placement, and extending the AI systems that run our day-to-day operations. We want someone who can both crunch the data and ship the code. What You'll Do Build and improve models that find profitable betting opportunities Develop automated/autonomous systems that place and manage bets across sportsbooks Help build AI-driven management tools (agents that draft messages, monitor accounts, and flag issues) Pull, clean, and analyze sports and odds data from APIs and websites Test ideas with real data and turn the ones that work into reliable, running systems What We're Looking For Strong Python (data analysis, automation, APIs) Experience with data science / machine learning, or a solid statistics foundation Comfortable working with messy real-world data (scraping, APIs, cleaning) Experience using and integrating AI/LLMs — the Claude API or similar is a strong plus Genuine interest in sports and sports betting Self-starter who can take an idea from data to a working system Comfortable with a flexible schedule Nice to Have Sports-betting, trading, or quantitative modeling experience Experience building bots or automation that interacts with websites Cloud / databases / building reliable long-running systems How to Apply Send us: A short intro about yourself and the most relevant project you've built (link to code/GitHub if you have it) Your highest educational qualification Your expected monthly salary (USD) subject must be witty with the word "LEBROWN CHEYMS" with it A 1–3 minutes video answering: Why should we hire you? If you were building a model to bet on one sport, which sport would you pick and what data would you look at first? We look forward to hearing from you!

About Us We're an arbitrage sports-betting company building the systems that find edges, place bets, and manage operations automatically. We use AI throughout the business and are investing heavily in models and automation. You'd be helping build the engine. The Role You'll develop the software and models behind our betting. That means building predictive sports-betting models, automating bet placement, and extending the AI systems that run our day-to-day operations. We want someone who can both crunch the data and ship the code. What You'll Do Build and improve models that find profitable betting opportunities Develop automated/autonomous systems that place and manage bets across sportsbooks Help build AI-driven management tools (agents that draft messages, monitor accounts, and flag issues) Pull, clean, and analyze sports and odds data from APIs and websites Test ideas with real data and turn the ones that work into reliable, running systems What We're Looking For Strong Python (data analysis, automation, APIs) Experience with data science / machine learning, or a solid statistics foundation Comfortable working with messy real-world data (scraping, APIs, cleaning) Experience using and integrating AI/LLMs — the Claude API or similar is a strong plus Genuine interest in sports and sports betting Self-starter who can take an idea from data to a working system Comfortable with a flexible schedule Nice to Have Sports-betting, trading, or quantitative modeling experience Experience building bots or automation that interacts with websites Cloud / databases / building reliable long-running systems How to Apply Send us: A short intro about yourself and the most relevant project you've built (link to code/GitHub if you have it) Your highest educational qualification Your expected monthly salary (USD) subject must have the word "LEBROWN CHEYMS" on it A 1–3 minute video (Non-negotiable) answering: 1. Why should we hire you? 2. If you were building a model to bet on one sport, which sport would you pick and what data would you look at first? Send your application here: sykhizzer.assistance@gmail.com We look forward to hearing from you!

Full job description

The role involves supporting Lucy, the Founder of small business RUN OF A KIND ( ---------- ) RUN OF A KIND is Birmingham’s first and only running tour company, now evolving into a wider running events and services provider focused on unique public and corporate running experiences. We’re looking for a highly organised, trustworthy and proactive Virtual Assistant to become a key part of the team, helping build the systems, marketing and operational foundations needed to scale RUN OF A KIND. This is not just an admin role. We want someone trustworthy who can listen and implement according to instructions, execute efficiently, improve systems, and help keep the business moving forward. Core Responsibilities Marketing & Content Support: Draft and schedule LinkedIn and Instagram content using our brand voice Repurpose content intelligently across: - Instagram - LinkedIn - Email campaigns in Squarespace - Website blogs Upload and schedule content via Buffer Design simple graphics, carousels and templates in Canva Support light community management: WhatsApp comments DMs engagement Events & Listings Set up events on Eventbrite (titles, descriptions, images, pricing, upsells) Add merch upsells and discount codes to Eventbrite Update events when details change Duplicate and adapt existing listings for new dates Schedule post-event follow-ups (thank you emails, review links) Sales & Client Support Follow up warm leads via email Respond to general enquiries Flag priority opportunities to Lucy Send proposals and quotes using templates Research leads and partnership opportunities Support outreach to: corporates residential communities universities sponsors Keep HubSpot CRM updated and organised Track pipeline and follow-up actions General Admin & Systems Organise files in Google Drive Maintain trackers, documents and dashboards in Notion / Google Sheets Create and improve SOPs and templates Support onboarding systems for team and events Complete light research and AI-assisted tasks using ChatGPT Systems You’ll Use Google Mail & Google Drive Squarespace Eventbrite Canva Instagram & LinkedIn Buffer Notion HubSpot CRM ChatGPT / AI tools What Success Looks Like: You help RUN OF A KIND become more organised, more efficient and more scalable. That means: Marketing goes out consistently Leads are followed up quickly Systems improve over time Lucy spends less time in admin and more time growing the business You’d Be a Great Fit If You: Are trustworthy, highly organised and efficient Prioritise tasks well and work fast Communicate clearly in excellent written English Love systems, checklists and improving processes Can follow a strong brand voice Spot gaps, errors and opportunities without being asked Are proactive and commercially minded Are comfortable with some overlap with UK working hours Bonus (Not Essential, But a Big Plus) Experience supporting founders or small businesses Strong understanding of content marketing Experience with CRM systems Comfortable using AI tools to improve productivity Interest in fitness, events or community-led brands Great sense of humour and positive energy Growth Opportunity I see this as a long-term role for the right person. RUN OF A KIND is in an exciting growth phase, and I’m looking for someone who wants to grow with the business, not just complete short-term tasks. Initially, this role will focus on admin, systems and marketing support, helping build strong foundations and freeing Lucy up to focus on growth. Over time, there is potential for the role to expand into a full-time role with greater responsibility depending on your strengths and interests, including areas such as: Marketing strategy CRM and automation Sales support Team operations Project management Leadership I’d love to find someone who feels genuinely excited about being part of the journey and helping build something special over the long term.

Full job description

About the Role Aurify is looking for a Creative AI Prompt Engineer to join our product team. You'll build and refine prompts and workflows across generative AI tools (Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, image/video/voice models, and more) to produce content with real viral potential. This role blends a sharp creative eye with technical prompt-crafting skill — you'll be the person who can look at a photo, a clip, or a concept and know whether it has what it takes to break out, then know exactly how to prompt the right AI tools to get there. What You'll Do • Build, test, and refine prompts and workflows across multiple AI tools (Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, image/video generation models, LLMs) to produce high-quality creative output • Identify and select source content (photos, clips, concepts) with strong viral potential • Develop repeatable prompt templates and workflows that the product team can use and scale • Experiment with new AI tools and models, evaluating them for creative quality, output reliability, and fit with product goals • Collaborate closely with the product team to turn ideas into AI-generated content pipelines • Iterate quickly based on performance data and creative feedback • Stay on top of emerging AI tools, models, and techniques in generative content (image, video, voice, audio) What We're Looking For • Fluent English (written and spoken) • Hands-on experience with prompt engineering across multiple AI tools — ideally including Higgsfield, ElevenLabs, or similar image/video/voice generation platforms • A strong creative instinct — you can spot what makes content shareable, trend-worthy, or viral • Comfort working iteratively: testing prompts, evaluating outputs, refining fast • Ability to build and document workflows/templates that others can reuse • Based in or willing to work full-time in Seoul Nice to Have • Experience with social media trends, short-form video, or viral content analysis • Basic scripting or API experience (Python, JavaScript) for automating AI workflows • Background in content creation, video editing, or digital marketing Why Join Aurify You'll be at the center of how Aurify builds AI-powered creative content from the ground up — with real ownership over tools, workflows, and creative direction, working directly with the product team. How to Apply Send the following to shiela@aurify.com: 1. A brief email explaining why you're a good fit for this role 2. Your resume 3. A portfolio showcasing relevant AI prompt/creative work (image, video, or content samples)

Arca angle

Full job description

About CaliaOS CaliaOS is an AI-powered growth platform built specifically for insurance agencies. We don't simply provide software—we implement systems that help agencies capture more opportunities, respond faster, book more appointments, retain more customers, and deliver a better customer experience. We're looking for an experienced GoHighLevel (GHL) Implementation Specialist who enjoys building systems, solving problems, and working directly with business owners to ensure every client launches successfully. Our Mission CaliaOS gives insurance agencies a system that works even when they aren't. Our platform captures more opportunities, follows up consistently, books more appointments, improves customer retention, and creates a better client experience through intelligent automation. As an Implementation Specialist, your responsibility is to deploy that system correctly. Every implementation should ensure the client launches with a complete, reliable foundation so CaliaOS can begin delivering results from day one. What You'll Do You will own the client implementation from kickoff through launch. Responsibilities include: * Lead onboarding and implementation meetings with business owners. * Learn how each agency operates and identify their highest-impact priorities. * Build and configure GoHighLevel CRM. * Import, organize, and clean customer data. * Remove duplicate contacts. * Standardize tags, custom fields, and pipeline stages. * Configure calendars and appointment booking. * Connect domains, email, phone numbers, forms, and websites. * Configure AI assistants, workflows, and automations. * Add quote links and customer resources where appropriate. * Test every workflow before launch. * Train clients on using their new system. * Document each implementation for future support. Every Implementation Begins With Data Cleanup Before any automation is activated, every client implementation must begin with: * CRM cleanup * Duplicate removal * Contact organization * Tag standardization * Pipeline cleanup * Calendar configuration * Communication routing Without clean data, automations cannot communicate correctly. Skills & Qualifications Required: * Proven GoHighLevel (GHL) experience. * CRM implementation experience. * Workflow and automation experience. * Pipeline management. * Calendar configuration. * Form and funnel setup. * Contact imports and CRM cleanup. * Strong troubleshooting skills. * Excellent organization. * Ability to communicate professionally with business owners. * Strong written and spoken English. Preferred: * Insurance industry experience. * AI automation experience. * Basic DNS knowledge (SPF, DKIM, domain verification). * Experience with email deliverability. * API and webhook knowledge. What Success Looks Like Every client launches with: * A clean CRM. * Organized contact data. * Proper tagging structure. * Working automations. * Connected calendars. * Verified email sending. * Connected phone system. * Tested workflows. * A fully functioning implementation ready to deliver results. Who We're Looking For We're looking for someone who thinks beyond clicking buttons. You should enjoy solving operational problems, building organized systems, and helping business owners become more efficient. You take pride in creating implementations that work correctly the first time and understand that every detail matters. Application Requirements To be considered, please submit: * Your resume. * A brief summary of your GoHighLevel experience. * Examples of GHL implementations, workflows, or automations you've built (if available). Include a 2–5 Minute Video Please record a short video covering: * Tell us about yourself. * Why are you interested in joining CaliaOS? * Describe your experience with GoHighLevel. * Walk us through a workflow or automation you've built. * How do you approach onboarding a new client? * What do you believe makes a great implementation experience? Applications without a video will not be considered. Why Join CaliaOS? You'll help build systems that make a real difference for insurance agencies across the country. Your work won't just configure software—it will create the operational foundation that allows our clients to serve customers better, recover more opportunities, and grow with confidence. If you enjoy building systems, working with technology, and delivering exceptional client experiences, we'd love to meet you.

Alex and Ani is an American jewelry brand rooted in purpose, positivity, and self-expression that designs expandable wire bangles, charm bracelets, necklaces, earrings, and rings that are intended to do more than adorn — they enlighten the mind and empower...

  • AI/build signals: llm
  • Build surface: dashboard, dashboards, repo, workflow, workflows, ghl, shopify
  • Code/build substrate: dashboard, dashboards, repo
  • Trainable language: va, fresh, sop, templates, learn
  • Scarcity/seniority signal: lead, manager, architect, expert
Arca angle
  • Full-time role: good fit for a dedicated Arca graduate placement.
Full job description

Alex and Ani is an American jewelry brand rooted in purpose, positivity, and self-expression that designs expandable wire bangles, charm bracelets, necklaces, earrings, and rings that are intended to do more than adorn — they enlighten the mind and empower the spirit. As we continue to grow and invest in our direct-to-consumer operations, we are building a best-in-class customer support function from the ground up and are looking for a Customer Support Operations Manager to be the architect of that effort — someone who will build a team of VAs afresh. It is REQUIRED for this role that you have: *A substantial history of working in a CPG / D2C setting *A history at a company managing a large number of SKUs *Experience building teams from the ground up and forming the key infrastructure for those teams *Experience managing at least ten people for more than two years RESPONSIBILITIES - Team Building & Leadership: *Build the customer support team from the ground up, including defining team structure, headcount planning, role design, and hiring. *Recruit, onboard, coach, and develop support agents and team leads, fostering a high-performance culture grounded in accountability and growth. *Conduct regular 1:1s, performance reviews, and career development conversations that genuinely invest in each team member's trajectory. *Lead by example — model the communication standards, customer empathy, and operational rigor you expect from your team. Operations, Infrastructure & Process Design: *Design and implement all foundational support infrastructure: workflows, escalation paths, ticketing configurations, knowledge bases, SOPs, and communication templates. *Build and maintain a Quality Assurance (QA) program, including calibration sessions, scoring rubrics, and feedback loops to continuously raise service standards. *Develop, document, and iterate on policies and procedures that ensure consistency, compliance, and a seamless customer experience across all channels. *Establish and maintain agent and team-level KPIs, dashboards, and reporting frameworks that provide real-time visibility into performance and emerging trends. *Proactively identify operational gaps and implement scalable solutions before they become systemic issues. Performance Management & Continuous Improvement: *Own all team performance metrics including CSAT, first response time, resolution time, quality scores, and productivity benchmarks. *Analyze performance data to surface patterns, identify root causes, and drive targeted coaching or process improvements. *Lead regular team reviews, QA calibrations, and retrospectives to ensure the team is improving consistently and collaboratively. *Serve as the primary escalation point for complex, sensitive, or high-priority customer issues, resolving them with professionalism and care. REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS - *Minimum 5 years of experience in customer support or customer experience roles, with at least 2–3 years in a management or team leadership capacity. *Demonstrated experience building a support team from the ground up — not inheriting an existing operation, but genuinely starting from scratch. *Proven track record of scaling support teams across different growth stages, adjusting processes, structures, and tooling as the team evolves. *Strong expertise in KPI design, reporting dashboards, and the metrics that matter most for a high-performing support organization. *Experience developing and owning QA programs, coaching frameworks, and structured performance management processes. *Deep familiarity with customer support tooling (e.g., Zendesk, Gorgias, Freshdesk, or similar platforms). *Exceptional written and verbal communication in English, with the ability to engage clearly and professionally across all levels. *Experience supporting an e-commerce, retail, or direct-to-consumer brand is strongly preferred. WHO YOU ARE - *A builder: You are energized by creating structure where there is none, and you know how to prioritize ruthlessly when everything feels urgent. *Autonomous and self-directed: You don’t need to be told what to do next. You identify the gaps, make a plan, and execute. *A genuine people developer: You invest in your team not because it’s on the checklist, but because it’s the most important thing you do. *Highly adaptable: You pick things up fast, adjust when the landscape changes, and never stop learning. *A multitasker with range: You can wear many hats — strategist, coach, analyst, escalation handler — and make it look seamless. *Data-informed but human-centered: You know your numbers and you also know that behind every ticket is a real person who deserves a real answer. TO APPLY - Please include your resume and the answers to these questions: 1. "Walk me through how you've managed customer support operations for a CPG brand selling direct-to-consumer — what platforms were you using, and how did you handle order-related issues like failed payments, shipping delays, or subscription problems specifically within Shopify or a similar storefront?" 2. "In a remote setup, how have you built and maintained quality and consistency across your support team when handling high-volume periods like product launches or promotional sales — and what tools or workflows did you rely on to stay aligned with the brand and fulfillment side of the business?" 3. "Tell me about a time a customer escalation exposed a gap in either the product, the fulfillment process, or the storefront experience — how did you document it, escalate it internally, and what changed as a result?" 4. "What tools and technologies have you used to manage orders in the D2C space?"

  • AI/build signals: ai tools
  • Build surface: website, websites, landing page, landing pages, front-end, repo, html, css
  • Code/build substrate: front-end, repo, html, css, javascript, api
  • Trainable language: va, sop, learn
